title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location
neg_text:
Lack of intimacy. The bathroom is separated in the room by means of glass panels, frosted, although not over the entire surface. Every little sound detail from the toilet is heard in the room. Sounds from adjacent rooms as well.
The range of TV channels excludes guests with conservative views. A restaurant space with an intimacy similar to that of a railway station hall. I do not recommend it to people looking for an intimate atmosphere.

title: We hoped for better experience
pos_text:
Great location, very very friendly staff.
Breakfast was nice, although I would like to see more local products.
neg_text:
This Puro Hotel had noticeable lower quality than other Puro locations I visited. Rooms were older and some facilities were surprisingly bad. For us the biggest issue was that our room was stuffy and hot regardless of the air conditioning setting. The hotel's intervention didn't improve the situation.
As a result we couldn't have a good sleep during our busy stay in Wroclaw.

title: Pleasant

neg_text: Parking - a disaster. Very tight, parking was complicated, especially in situations where someone was entering and another vehicle was leaving at the same time. Plus problems with the parking meter. All this for 100 PLN per day. Breakfasts are served at the adjacent facility (during the hotel restaurant renovation) do not meet Puro standards, also a big disappointment.

neg_text: Bathroom design is silly, with no privacy.

title: When the renovation is finished, 10/10, now I wouldn't recommend it to anyone, unless someone is getting there at 5 in the morning

neg_text: There is information about the modernization works of the restaurant that have been ongoing for 2 months. But we were very disappointed, because it turned out that the guest paying 350 PLN/night without meals after discounts had a view of a toy truck, and was woken up by HAMMERING AND DRILLING. Some kind of joke. Room on the 2nd floor, and noise from waking up to check-out :)

title: Pleasant

title: Pleasant
pos_text: Location, decor

title: Excellent staff, modern, central hotel but poor sound insulation for Friday DJ sets.
pos_text:
Modern and nicely designed hotel with very friendly, supportive and professional staff. Central location and paid parking on site - which
made arrival very convenient. Good communication on the phone too.
neg_text: What put a dent in the one night stay was the Friday DJ set (organized by the hotel itself) that ended at 22.00. We had a room on the 3rd floor with windows facing the hotels yard and the DJ set was so loud, that even with closed windows you could just hear loud music in the room. Not just the bass even, the set. As someone who was looking to rest up in the evening after the day of travel - needles to say I was upset about but a) too tired to go downstairs and make this my problem b) the set wouldn’t be stopped just because one person can’t rest up. I informed reception next day that it really wasn’t ok.

title: Weekend trip to Wroclaw
pos_text: The bed was comfortable. The hotel was in a great location, there are some lovely vars and restaurants nearby and only a few minutesxwalk tontgexsquare. The decor was pleasant and tge hotel was clean. There is a beautiful garden area which is very peaceful. The staff were very friendly and helpful. There was a good selection for breakfast. Great to have free use of the drinks machine in the reception area.
neg_text:
Everything in the room is controlled by a tablet, in the first room we went in the tablet did not work properly so we had no air conditioning and we were extremely hot in the night. The staff were very helpful and we changed rooms which was better. The air conditioning definitely worked better (especially on night 3) however the bathroom underfloor heating didn't work in either room meaning the floor of the toilet and sink area stayed wet too.
Even the do not disturb function didn't work on either tablet, we just got an error message.

title: Passable
pos_text:
The staff and location of the hotel are wonderful.
There is no need to pay for the animal.
There is indoor parking nearby that is much cheaper than at the hotel.
neg_text:
The idea of controlling rooms via a smart home has been implemented extremely poorly.
In our case, the air conditioner did not work (settings on the tablet, windows and doors closed - nothing helped).
At the same time, the bed is incredibly soft with a very warm blanket on a hot day. As you can imagine, it was impossible to sleep.
Also, the main light in the room did not turn on for more than 2 seconds, after which it automatically went out.
The lighting in the toilet is activated automatically when movement is detected. But the problem is that the toilet walls are glass. So if your dog moves near the toilet at night, the light comes on. This cannot be turned off.

title: We definitely won't be back
pos_text: A plus for the location.
neg_text:
Cons:
- very small rooms, lack of space,
- small bathroom, poorly constructed, because the shower immediately spills water all over the surface
- no bathrobes, slippers
- uncomfortable beds, very soft pillows
- poor breakfast - especially for this standard of hotel. No products for allergy sufferers. The staff does not know what the ingredients of the products displayed on the buffet are.

title: The price-performance ratio leaves much to be desired.
pos_text: The breakfast and the location of the hotel.
neg_text: We stayed at the hotel for 5 nights and not once was the room cleaned or the garbage disposed of, despite pointing this out at reception. We had to dispose of the garbage ourselves every two days. We had to ask if they could provide us with another roll of toilet paper. On the day of departure, we were offered a concession and were to be reimbursed amount X, but unfortunately, four weeks later, we have not received a refund. We are very disappointed about this.

title: I won't go back there, Puro yes, but not in Wro
pos_text: location, cosmetics
neg_text: I was there 10 years ago and nothing has changed, Puro in Poznań is much better. Christmas breakfast - for PLN 300 for 4 people ... around the corner for half the price and great flavors. small rooms, no kettle in the room, no bottle opener

title: A Beautiful Object with Wasted Potential
pos_text: Location, decor, architecture of the facility
neg_text:
Beautiful facility and location, but I have the impression that everyone works there as a punishment. Well, maybe apart from the lady at the reception who welcomed us and the gentleman from room service. The organization in the restaurant is completely broken. Every second thing was missing at breakfast, the staff was wandering around the room with grimacing faces, not noticing that basic products were missing from the buffet. Drawing attention causes reactions, but with great grace, and even at that moment there is no smile on the faces. The preparation time for a meal from the breakfast menu was so long that we lost our hunger.

I would like to emphasize that the problem does not concern one waiter or waitress. The atmosphere was as if someone had just been killed, and what's more, it was probably the hotel guests who were the killers.

If a fish rots from the head down, then the head is in a state of complete decomposition.

Hotels in large cities fall into two categories.
1. It was great, next time I won't look for anything else.
2. It was okay, but I definitely won't be back. but how to do it to remember it.

This is the second category.

NOTE: The rooms have bathroom architecture that requires a couple traveling together to be in a state of mind where they can use the toilet next to each other. This may be a problem for some.

Aaa... and to this day I don't know what it means"The poached eggs are gone but they are still fried" in the context of a meal made fresh and ordered from the menu. What ended there? Water in the pot? Well, it's an egg if it can be fried.

title: Great location but the hotel itself was a bit of a disappointment
pos_text: Very good location, nice and helpful staff, quite well soundproofed, comfortable bed.
neg_text: Very cold room: unable to adjust room temperature to suit your needs - unfortunately, despite increasing the heat level the temperature still remained the same - very cold. Blackout curtains. No wardrobe/instead of a wardrobe, a few hangers and 3 small, completely darkened shelves. The bathroom is literally in the room, separated only by a smoked glass window and a curtain, which means you can hear everything from there. The shower was open to the bathroom, literally flooding it, the water was so heavily chlorinated that the smell of chlorine lingered in the room for a very long time, and at the same time there was no way to air the room. No possibility to use the offer of streaming services despite the information provided on Booking.com

title: Basic but nice.
pos_text:
The highlight of my stay was undoubtedly the hotel's exceptional customer service. The staff members were not only friendly but also went out of their way to assist with any inquiries or requests I had. Their genuine willingness to help created a welcoming atmosphere that enhanced my overall stay.

The cleanliness of the hotel was another notable aspect. The attention to detail in maintaining a spotless environment did not go unnoticed. Whether it was the lobby, the corridors, or my room, everything was impeccably clean, contributing to a sense of comfort and well-being.

The comfort of the bed was exceptional. After a day of exploring the city, sinking into the plush mattress was a blissful experience. The quality of the bedding and pillows added to the overall coziness, ensuring a restful night's sleep.
neg_text:
One of the disappointments was the value for money concerning toiletries. The limited selection provided in the room did not align with the hotel's pricing. Guests typically expect a more extensive array of toiletries, and this aspect felt lacking in comparison to other establishments.

Another inconvenience was the absence of an ironing board in the room. While the hotel may offer an iron upon request, having this basic amenity readily available would have added to the convenience of the stay.

The quality of the hairdryer provided was below expectations. A more professional-grade appliance would have been appreciated, as the one in the room seemed outdated and less effective than anticipated.

Furthermore, the inability to connect personal devices using an HDMI cable due to the unavailability of an adapter was a frustrating limitation. In an era where connectivity is crucial, this oversight could be a significant inconvenience for guests who rely on such amenities.

title: The quality doesn't match the price
pos_text: Initially everything looked ok, appearance, location good standard.
neg_text: We feel cheated due to the double card authorization, we were brushed off and the bank sent us twice for the mistake of charging us. The bank informed us that the hotel was at fault and that the fault lay with them - fortunately, our bank will be clarifying the matter with them directly. The second topic is an additional bed for a child. I asked several times and she was still not there when I arrived. The third one is bullshit, but when I hear that my guests were supposed to go to the bar to heat up some hot water for the baby and get some baby bottles, it makes me cringe…For such a price and such a renowned hotel - a bit too much of a letdown. To sum up - we feel disappointed because at this standard the customer has the right to expect better execution of the services offered by PURO.

title: Zdecydowanie najsłabszy hotel z sieci PURO, który znamy
pos_text: Lokalizacja
neg_text:
Hotel Puro we Wrocławiu jest zdecydowanie najsłabszym, w którym byliśmy z tej sieci. Pokoje i łazienki malutkie, co można by jeszcze było zrozumieć, gdyby nie fakt, ze po każdym prysznicu łazienka była cała w wodzie. Garaż bardzo ciasny, wiec właściciele bardzo dużych aut lub niepewni kierowcy mogą mieć problemy.
Najgorszym jednak, w tym wszystkim było to, że pokój był pełen pająków, największe wielkości około 6 cm, mąż wybijał po kolei jak leci, niestety ciagle pojawiały się nowe. O otworzeniu okien nie mogło być mowy, gdyż cała ściana na zewnątrz była w tych pająkach, zakładam wiec, ze może podczas sprzątania wchodzą do pokoju, ewentualnie przez jakaś wentylacje, ale dla osoby z panicznym lękiem pobyt zamienił się w koszmar 😅
Pokój w teorii miał być posprzątany w ciągu dnia, niestety jedyne co zastaliśmy to złożona na łóżku pościel - nic innego było nieruszone, nie wspominając, że obsługa nie dała nam nawet świeżych kubków, tylko zostawili nam brudne z resztkami herbaty z poprzedniego wieczoru. Klimatyzacja ledwo działała, a jak już wspomniałam otworzenie okien nie wchodziło w grę, wiec w pokoju było bardzo duszno (temperatury na zewnątrz powyżej 30 stopni).

pos_text: W obiekcie najbardziej podobała mi się wyremontowana część lobby i to tyle. Zupełnie inaczej miejsce prezentuje się w internecie niż na żywo. Jest o wiele mniejsze i nie wykończone.
neg_text: Warunki oferowane przez hotel były, moim zdaniem, nieadekwatne do ceny. Puro jest pozycjonowane jako obiekt z wyższej półki, dlatego moje oczekiwania były większe - niestety zostały one niespełnione. Pokoje okazały się bardzo małe i znacząco odbiegały od tego, co prezentowały zdjęcia. Korytarze są ciasne i sprawiają nieprzyjemne wrażenie, a umieszczone na ścianach tablety, które miały podkreślać nowoczesność, dają raczej poczucie nadmiernej kontroli ze strony personelu. Dodatkowo obsługa sprawiała wrażenie mało zaangażowanej i niezadowolonej z pracy. Całość oceniam zdecydowanie negatywnie.

title: No clue why this hotel is highly rated on Booking
pos_text: The location is great. The rooms inside were clean.
neg_text:
- MOST IMPORTANTLY: the AC DOESN'T WORK PROPERLY; we were there when the temperature outside was about 26-27 degrees C (so not even as high as it gets), but it was practically impossible to have the temperature in the room get below 24 degrees C; as you may see in the point below, it seems to be the case for all the rooms; we ended up swapping hotels after one night because of it
- the stuff: while the stuff was very kind and helpful in standard situations (showing us to the room, check-in, etc.), when there appeared an issue (AC not working), they were not helpful and quite unprofessional; when we complained about the A/C, one staffer told us that ""it was normal that the temperature in the room could not be further decreased, as the day was sunny and the windows in the room were very large", another said that "the A/C system is just different from other hotels", yet another offered us a different room and after our requests and inquiries has assured us that she's verified it personally and that the other room definitely had a properly working AC, so my husband has packed all our things and moved them to that room (on a different floor) just to discover that in that room the A/C had an even bigger problem: it was not even possible to turn it on;
- cleanliness: although the rooms were clean, the corridor leading to them and the lift were not; the carpets were very stained
- room setup: there was this strange setup where the bathroom was divided from the room by a glass wall; we didn't understand the idea to begin with (as probably it's also more costly to furnish), however, it could be done in an acceptable way (that we've seen in a different hotel) with adding a light-blocking curtain to the setup.

title: Rozczarowanie
pos_text: Od początku lubię hotele Puro i zawsze są moim pierwszym wyborem czy służbowo czy prywatnie . Poza jednym w Łodzi odwiedziłem wszystkie . Design nowoczesność i świetne położenie w mieście to lubię najbardziej
neg_text: Pierwszy raz spotkało mnie takie rozczarowanie związane z tym hotelem , korzystam z hoteli wiele lat w różnych lokalizacjach , zarówno służbowo jak i prywatnie i zawsze były moim pierwszym wyborem przy rezerwacjach , raptem dobę przed przyjazdem okazało się iż mimo że rezerwacji dokonałem na długo przed planowanym przyjazdem , aktualna cena jest dwukrotnie niższa niż ta którą ja zapłaciłem . Możliwe że polityka firmy jest taka , natomiast reakcja personalu w obiekcie na pytanie dlaczego jest taka sytuacja była opryskliwa i bardzo niemiła . Nawet jeśli nie mają na to wpływu uprzejmość jest mile widziana . Generalnie obsługa poniżej klasy obiektu , parking który zazwyczaj w hotelach Puro jest swoistym rodzajem wyzwaniem dla kierowców , ciasny z okropnym wjazdem i mikroskopijnymi miejscami przy każdym wyjeździe wymagał powrotu do recepcji bo karta niby powinna działać ale tak nie było . Z kartą do pokoju podobnie . Tablet w pokoju żyje swoim życiem . Nie mam problemu z funkcjonowaniem w świecie technologii , jednak ustawienie klimatyzacji kilka razy powracało do ustawień bazowych mimo zadanej każdorazowo temperatury , w nocy dwukrotnie wymagało ponownego ustawiania ponieważ był wyjątkowy upał w pokoju ciężko było wytrzymać . Inne funkcje tabletu też działają tak sobie , pomijając fakt iż menu przywitało osobę o innych danych niż moje i mimo interwencji w recepcji tak pozostało do końca . Po raz pierwszy pobyt to wielkie rozczarowanie , zazwyczaj wyjeżdżałem w euforii z chęcią powrotu i rekomendowałem hotel wśród znajomych , tym razem sytuacja była zupełnie inna . Zdziwienie wzbudza również brak stacji ładowania pojazdów elektrycznych w garażu , w hotelu który jest wysoko oceniany i doba kosztuje 800 zł chyba można oczekiwać wysokich standardów

title: Poor
pos_text: Good location, tasty breakfasts, mostly friendly service
neg_text: Due to the hotel's error, my card and account were charged. I was hoping that the day after reporting this unpleasant situation I would receive a refund of my funds, or at least an explanation of the situation. Unfortunately not, they are still analyzing and I was unable to pay for my purchases at the store. The service is usually nice, except for one receptionist. Overall, the price-quality ratio is unfavorable.

title: Disappointing
pos_text: Location, cleanness
neg_text: We booked room for 2 adults and 11y/o kid, however, the room was equipped with one bed for 2 adults maximum. The hotel offered to fit in our kid on this same bed, and informed that Booking.com allows to offer this type of room to families traveling with kids of any age without any disclosure that a separate bed for kids of this age are not available. I drove 12h my car before accomodation at the hotel in the evening, and had to find solution to accommodate my kid with an acquaintance in Wroclaw. I'm a regular traveller and that's first time I faced this matter, looking much like a cheap scam. I hope Booking.com is not got involved in that.

title: Very poor value for money
pos_text: The location is good in the center, the breakfast is very good and plentiful, and the garden is a nice place to have a drink or relax.
neg_text: A terrible price-performance ratio. Very small room, very uncomfortable, the bathroom was not clean enough. There was nothing in the fridge and the air conditioning was very ineffective. The room overlooked the garden, on the first floor, which has spotlights all night, and during the day, although beautiful, everyone can see you if you don't have the curtains closed. For the price, it seems high-end, but it doesn't correspond to reality. Not worth it, in my opinion.

title: Puro Hotel boasts a prime location and impressive design, accompanied by inviting social areas. However, the experience
pos_text: Puro Hotel is a true gem with its amazing design and comfortable social zones. The staff is super friendly and professional, ensuring a warm welcome. Its unbeatable location allows easy access to all the attractions in the old city. The restaurant offers great food.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, my stay at the hotel room was highly disappointing due to several reasons. Firstly, the air conditioning system was ineffective, making it impossible to regulate the temperature adequately. Despite adjusting the settings, the room remained uncomfortably hot, especially in the sunny room with temperatures reaching +28 degrees Celsius. At night, the AC cooled only the immediate area around the ceiling, leaving the rest of the room stuffy and unpleasant. The staff insisted that the AC was functioning correctly, but the noticeable temperature difference between the corridors and the room was undeniable.
The lighting system, controlled through a digital panel, was also problematic. It was impossible to completely turn off the lights, leading to unexpected illumination in the bathroom during the night due to motion detection. This constant activation of lights disturbed sleep, particularly if there were two occupants in the room, as the bathroom had a glass wall.
Moreover, the bright display of the digital panel itself became a hindrance to sleep, as there was no option to reduce the brightness. The continuous glow emitted from the panel disrupted the room's ambiance throughout the night.
Lastly, considering the hotel's level, it was disappointing to find that the basic room did not include a refrigerator. This amenity is typically expected in accommodations of this caliber.
Overall, my experience in the room was one of the worst I have encountered. The ineffective air conditioning, faulty lighting system, and lack of a fridge were significant drawbacks.

title: Okay for a short stay!
pos_text:
The hotel has modern furnishings. The rooms are also functional. The beds are comfortable, but very worn out.
Free parking is available in front of the hotel, if one is free. We had parked on the other side of the Oder river.
neg_text:
The hotel staff are young, but not very hardworking. Many people showed a lack of enthusiasm for work, and they also left their friendliness at home.
The breakfast buffet was no longer fully replenished even one hour before the end. The staff had to be reminded about many different missing items (e.g., cups). It was also unpleasant that, for example, spilled jam wasn't removed from the buffet!
Dinner consisted of a starter and a main course. This is quite a surprise, as there was no map to be seen anywhere!
On the first evening we were informed that water, apple juice and/or orange juice would be included at no extra charge. On the second evening, we were simply given water, and on the third evening there was nothing at all. We weren't even asked!
It was also strange that the restaurant was closed on Sundays. When we inquired about dinner, we were informed that dinner would be provided for guests who had already booked half-board. When we sat at the table, a small salad was placed in front of us. When asked again if that was all, the answer was that there were no waiters today and that this was all that was available in the kitchen. And actually, nobody really knows for sure.

title: good hotel only for one night because the next days you forgot
pos_text: the position
neg_text:
the hotel's philosophy (ADD IT TO THE SITE) which, under the pretext of adhering to an environmentalist plan, did not tidy up, clean or change the linen for four days (even though the website states daily cleaning). On the third day, in the evening, when I asked the reason, at reception I was told that the cleaning of the room and the change of bathroom linen (two shower towels and one hand towel for two people), although included in the price, was only upon request due to the hotel's participation in an energy saving plan and that they would take care of it the following day (they hand-delivered me two shower towels and only one hand towel which was to be used by two people). The next day (the fourth) the room was neither cleaned nor tidied and following my complaints to the floor cleaner, the room was cleaned and tidied in the late afternoon. These features of the hotel services (absurd because I can understand the change of linen, but what energy savings does the tidying of the room refer to?). Uncomfortable bed with springs that are barely supportive.
The staff at the reception, never a greeting or a smile

Chciałem zjeść kolację w restauracji i otrzymać jedną fakturę za nocleg i konsumpcję, ale pani poinformowała mnie, że jest to niemożliwe. Po dłuższej rozmowie zaproponowała, żebym zarezerwował pokój przez booking i zapłacił kartą, wtedy się uda (tak zrobiłem). Przy wystawianiu faktury, w recepcji był pan i powiedział, że da się wystawić jedną fakturę bez wcześniejszej opłaty przez booking, tylko przy meldowaniu muszę wybrać z menu co będę jadł i od razu to doliczą... A few days earlier, they had already tried to withhold a similar, albeit lower, amount, and after requesting information through booking, the responses arrived, albeit rarely, but they weren't clear, and after a while, I didn't even receive a response.
In addition to this: toilet seat stained with urine NOT ours and even though I reported it as soon as I entered the room at reception they limited themselves to apologizing but no one cleaned it that same day or even the next day, I had to clean it myself.
The shower had no curtain or wall, directly on the floor, with the risk of a nearby hair dryer getting wet and causing serious injury. Towels were stained and few in number. title: Hotel for the patient. The hotel's 3 stars are a misunderstanding. Not for this money.
pos_text: On the plus side, the location is great (11/10), perfect for exploring the city on foot. Tasty breakfasts, quite a wide selection, good coffee. Elevator. Aesthetically decorated hotel. The bathroom is fine.
neg_text: At first glance, it might seem that the hotel is OK, but the details "put it down" my stay. When I got there, it turned out that I was not assigned the room I requested (not facing the street), in addition, a broken fan was constantly humming in the room and could not be turned off. Here everything was saved, because they changed my room - not facing the street and with a working fan (or so I thought, because later it turned out that, for a change, it was hardly blowing at all). It seemed that it would be 9-10/10 now, unfortunately what immediately caught my attention was the internal door in my room, as you can easily guess - connecting my room with another one. While the first night the adjacent room seemed empty, unfortunately on the second night, as expected, the loud family watching TV around 1 a.m. of the neighbors outside the door prevented me from sleeping properly. Perhaps it wouldn't have been audible if our rooms had been separated by a wall, but unfortunately, as we've already established, it was the door :). Luckily they were at least closed,.. Since it was the last night of my stay, I gave up on another conversation with the staff, but what kind of standard is this at this price in a hotel? A completely incomprehensible situation. I booked in advance. In turn, the previous day, when I was returning from sightseeing, my room card suddenly stopped working and they simply replaced it without a word of explanation? Poor communication from staff. The gentleman didn't speak to me at all, while one of the ladies spoke so quickly and unclearly that I had trouble understanding her... Are guests a sad necessity? It is also worth mentioning the wonderful green "wardrobes" for clothes that are so narrow that the clothes have to stick out through those funny "windows" like Nussbeisser chocolate-covered nuts.

title: An unpleasant experience
pos_text: Location. The location is absolutely ok.
neg_text: In the room we were given, the bed had broken boards. It's a shame that no one did anything about it when the room was being prepared before my arrival, and it was certainly visible after removing the bedding. We only noticed it when we went to bed, but since it was late we decided we would somehow get through the night on that bed. The next morning I reported the bed to the reception. I asked for a room change. Unfortunately, the lady at the reception said she couldn't change the room because the hotel was 100% booked, so I suggested changing the hotel. The lady said she would contact the hotel manager and call him, and she noted the bed failure. After reporting, we left the hotel. No one called during the day, so we assumed that perhaps the bed had been repaired or replaced, or that a place in another room had become available. Imagine our surprise when we returned to the hotel in the evening and found that nothing had been done about our case. The bed was just as broken before we left as it was when we returned. My second intervention at the reception desk and a conversation with the manager resulted in the bed being replaced. Of course, when I first reported it at the reception desk, the lady didn't inform anyone about it, let alone the manager. However, the hotel showed no sense of guilt, no compensation - not even in the form of a free breakfast, not even a simple apology from the receptionist who ignored the matter. I know that 3 stars is not 5, but still, some standard is expected from a 3-star hotel, especially in such an important matter. On my part, there is still a bad taste in my mouth, I will definitely avoid this hotel on future trips.

title: Lepiej dołożyć i wybrać 4 gwiazdkowy hotel
neg_text:
Hotel teoretycznie przyjazny psom. Teoretycznie. Idąc na śniadanie z małym pieskiem zostaliśmy zaatakowani przez innego gościa hotelu cytuję: „w domu psy, w restauracji psy, to nie jest miejsce dla psów” Panu nie spodobało się, że pies jest na półmetrowej smyczy obok naszego stolika i szczekał bo się cieszył widząc moich rodziców. Następnie zaczął wyzywać słownie mnie i moją mamę będąc przy tym bardzo agresywny. Po podejściu pracownika obsługi oddaliliśmy się do stolika. Wtedy podszedł do nas pracownik z prośbą ZAMKNIĘCIA psa w pokoju. Zapytałam czy jest poważny twierdząc, że zamknę szczeniaka samego w obcym pomieszczeniu bo jakiemuś panu, który wybrał hotel z wizytówką „przyjaznemu psom” przeszkadza mój pies. W całym tym zamieszaniu agresor tej całej sytuacji nie został ani wyproszony ani upomniany. Zapłaciłam za śniadanie, którego nie mogłam zjeść po tej całej sytuacji.
Na codzień mieszkam w Niemczech gdzie hotele nie muszą reklamować się jako przyjazne zwierzętom, a one jednak są wszędzie mile widziane i przede wszystkim nie są znikąd wypraszane - nawet te bardzo duże psy.
Ogólnie stan pokoju nie odpowiada standardom hoteli 3 gwiazdkowych.
Jednakże dziękujemy firmie Dolina Noteci za prezent powitalny w formie karmy dla Leni. Do wymówki „nie odpowiadamy za zachowanie gości” dopowiem, że odpowiadają Państwo za swoją relację. Jako kobieta atakowana przez dużego mężczyznę nie czułam się bezpiecznie. Wychodzi na to, że nawet bezpieczeństwo gości nie zostało zapewnione. Gdzie agresor powinien zostać wyproszony.

Niestety hotelu polecić nie możemy.

He then started verbally abusing me and my mother and was very aggressive. After the staff member approached us, we moved away to the table. Then an employee approached us and asked us to LOCK the dog in the room. I asked if he was serious, claiming that I would lock the puppy alone in a strange room because of some gentleman who chose a hotel with a "dog-friendly" business card. my dog is bothering me. In all this confusion, the aggressor of this whole situation was neither asked to leave nor reprimanded. I paid for breakfast, which I couldn't eat after all this.
I live in Germany where hotels don't have to advertise themselves as pet-friendly, yet they are welcome everywhere and, above all, they are not asked to leave - even very large dogs.
Overall, the condition of the room does not meet the standards of a 3-star hotel.
However, we would like to thank the Dolina Noteci company for the welcome gift of food for Leni.
Unfortunately, we cannot recommend the hotel.

title: Dziecko-widmo

neg_text: Jestem bardzo rozczarowana pobytem. Na szczęście był krótki. Wybór podyktowany lokalizacją i to chyba jedyny atut tego miejsca. Śniadanie bardzo przeciętne (smak i wybór), w połowie jego trwania połowa dań się kończy i nie jest już uzupełniana, ekspres psujący się, wielokrotnie wzywana była do niego obsługa. Podejście hotelu do dzieci karygodne. Rezerwacja dokonana przez Booking dla 2 dorosłych i 9-letniego dziecka. Zaproponowano nam pokój z dużym podwójnym łóżkiem i na takie się zdecydowaliśmy z pełną świadomością, że będziemy spać we trójkę. Natomiast nie spodziewaliśmy się, że zostaniemy potraktowani, jakby dziecka z nami nie było (oczywiście poza podpisaniem odpowiednich oświadczeń dot. małoletnich). Już w recepcji dziecko zostało usunięte z rezerwacji, ponieważ ograniczenia systemowe nie pozwalały na zameldowanie. W pokoju wszystko przygotowane dla dwóch osób: dwie poduszki, dwa ręczniki, dwa kubki na herbatę itd. Gdy poprosiliśmy o dodatkową poduszkę dla dziecka, bo jednak głowy mamy trzy i trudno to obejść to usłyszeliśmy, że hotel ma pełne obłożenie i nie mają dodatkowych poduszek. Gdyby były wolne pokoje to obsługa przyniosłaby poduszkę stamtąd, ale niestety, nic nie mają. Kierownik niedostępny, nawet telefonicznie. Dowiedzieliśmy się też, że to nie pierwsza taka sytuacja, personel obsługujący zgłasza podobne sprawy wyżej, jednak niczego to nie zmienia. A ściany windy obklejone ulotkami promującymi ekologię, przyjazność dla dzieci i zwierząt, na samym środku zasady generalne opisujące standardy chroniące dzieci, np. "Każdy gość hotelu, w tym dziecko, powinien być traktowany z należytym szacunkiem i poszanowaniem jego godności". Tymczasem dzieci w hotelu są niewidzialne. Czysta hipokryzja. Serdecznie nie polecam.

Unfortunately, other guests had the same experience. We wanted a second blanket and a second pillow, but this wish was denied and we were left sitting and ignored. It wasn't until Saturday morning, when a new receptionist arrived, that our requests were immediately fulfilled - even with a smile on her face. We booked a few weeks ago for just under €260 per room, so why did we have to pay €290 on site?
The rooms were not perfectly clean; there was dirt stuck under the chair and the water didn't drain in the shower. The rooms are very noisy, which didn't bother us too much - but other guests also mentioned this to Julia, and she just said: "Everyone feels that way, that's just how it is." - You can't treat guests like that! There was a wait for breakfast, and the queue kept getting longer. Barely 10 tables for a large number of guests - that's nowhere near enough! In short: we returned home very disappointed and dissatisfied! And Julia urgently needs training on how to deal with guests! Not every day is perfect and we all have bad moods sometimes, but at work you should be competent enough to hide that. We felt very unwelcome in her presence!

Cold, therefore, in a figurative sense, but especially, as far as we are concerned, in a literal sense… We arrived around 10 pm and at the time we didn't realize it, but our room was NOT HEATED… even though the outside temperatures were below freezing…. Incredible but true… I couldn't take a shower. I found the radiators were at 0 so I turned the radiators on and the air conditioning on heating mode. My wife went to bed fully dressed; she caught a cold. It was after 2.5 hours that it warmed up (the ceilings are very high, the windows too and it was unbearable, I was only able to take a shower around 3 am). I went to point it out afterwards at reception and I had to insist that the person take note and pass on my complaint.
The next day I went to reception to see the manager but they just gave me a form to fill out with the manager's email address…. I complained to reception, however, and all they could think to say was that she slept with the window open at night…. As if I cared to know… I am very disappointed and do not recommend this hotel, except in spring and summer.
Nothing there is warm, literally or figuratively.

title: Peaceful, Nice, Clean, Pleasant
pos_text:
The meeting in Wrocław ran over, so instead of dreaming of the highway at 11 p.m., I ended up at the DIKUL Hotel. Last minute booking – and bingo! A huge room, impeccable cleanliness, and a receptionist who greeted you so kindly that you forgot about the whole day at work.

In the morning – the view from the window is worthy of an advertisement for Wrocław, even though the weather is rather “autumn depression”.

For comparison, I recently slept in a 5-star hotel, The Granary La Suite, for six times the price and… seriously? DIKUL wins with cleanliness and politeness of service.

To sum up: luxury is not just about gold door handles – sometimes it's just a good night's sleep and a sincere smile at the reception desk. I recommend!
neg_text: The bathroom lacks basic things – a toothbrush and toothpaste, shower gel, or even a little cream. When you make a spontaneous booking for one night, you really feel the lack of such details. The hotel has GREAT/HUGE/GIGANTIC potential, but it feels like it's missing the right direction. Looking at this hotel, I see a place that – with appropriate development and consistent management – can easily compete with premium facilities. All it takes is a little strategic approach to customer experience to make DIKUL your number one choice.

The bidet was damaged and was flooding the bathroom. The staff solved it temporarily, but the bad taste remained.

title: Would not stay again.. many other accomodations in better locations/value for money
pos_text:
The old building that has been renovated to create this hotel.
The staff were friendly, one lady was particularly helpful (the lady on front desk the next morning after our arrival)
neg_text:
Many things.
* BIG CONCERN.. we had ordered an Uber one morning for 6:30am pick up. When we arrived down stairs and waited inside for the Uber to arrive, we realised the front door was locked (large wooden doors). There was no button to open them or any information how to open them. I become quite panicked and was trying everything to get outside. It was a terrible feeling to feel trapped with no idea of how to get out.
It was then a young lady came and unlocked the door with a large key.
(Big concern as there are no fire evacuation instructions or any emergency instructions (not in polish or English to be seen anywhere).

* Definitely not catered for the English speaking traveler (not one tv channel catered for in English speaking, not even the ability to use subtitles)
* Location: while in an okay location it is NOT on the old town market square. It is on the very outer of the old town and about 5 large blocks (walking) to the actual market square.
* Two sachets of tea, two sachets of coffee left for one week staying at this property. NO milk. When asked for Milk they said they did not have any. We had to buy our own.
* House cleaning is at an extra charge (we were fortunate to get 1 free clean due to the length of our stay. This should be mentioned in their profile.
* As the room was not serviced daily the dirt and dust and hair built up quickly on the floorboards. It become quite dirty to walk around in. A broom and dustpan would have been useful. The rooms are large and a lot of floor space.
*No hand towels left and had to use the bath mat to wipe hands on. Towels were old and ratty on the ends.
* When asking for One extra pillow, they said they didn’t know if they had any??? 24 hours later they delivered one.

We booked 7nights stay and left on day 5. We realised there were far better value for money stays, closer to the market square and with better facilities/amenities.

title: Stay with obstacles
pos_text:
The breakfast was plentiful and good. The location is very central. Just a few hundred meters to the market square.
The room was very large, as was the bathroom, and both were very clean. The heating worked well too.
neg_text:
The key card didn't work. The parking garage, which had spaces for hotel guests, was located on a one-way street where the hotel was also situated. However, the parking garage was a few meters in front of the hotel, so you couldn't turn around and drive straight in, but had to go forward through half the city and make a loop to get into the one-way street. For me, it happened at night and in the rain, so it was very tiring. Then there were several entrances to parking garages next to each other. The description of the parking lot was only in Polish. I encountered several cars reversing out of the various underground parking garages, all looking for the right spot, just like me. The whole thing took me almost 2 hours. It was said to be a secure parking garage, but I didn't even need the key because everything was always open.
Then I decided to stay another day and asked if there was a room available, perhaps even mine? The receptionist said no. Only one with a lower standard, with only one bed. I said it was no problem, since I was traveling alone anyway. Then she said that I would have to check out of my current room and could only check back in at 3 pm, and that I would have to wait until then. That made no sense to me, of course, as I wanted to see the city during the day. There was absolutely no willingness to store my suitcases for that long. Overall, both receptionists were neither friendly nor helpful.

pos_text: The location and parking right next to the hotel (paid) are definitely a plus. We managed to stay overnight at a promotional price, so the value for money was definitely a plus. The building itself has been restored and well-maintained from the outside.
neg_text: Unfortunately, we didn't like the room, we didn't feel comfortable there and we wouldn't recommend it to anyone. It was obvious that his glory days were behind him. The bathroom in particular, although relatively clean, is very worn and needs renovation. The bedspreads were torn and stained in places. After turning on the blinds with the remote control, we were covered in a cloud of dust; it seemed no one had been there for a long time. At the promotional price, it's worth it for one night, but I definitely wouldn't stay longer.

title: A disappointment, but in a good location
pos_text: ...a truly excellent location. Hassle-free luggage storage before and after your stay (generally the staff was rather nice and helpful). The breakfasts smelled nice but we didn't buy them so it's hard to say for sure. It's nice that there's a patio behind the hotel that you can use
neg_text:
The patio is clearly not monitored (or the curfew is not enforced) because throughout our stay we were disturbed by loud conversations and music until very late hours.
For information, the review is for a double room with a shared bed.
To start with we were given a card and a key. Strange and not very convenient, the card only turns on the electricity in the room, the key only allows you to enter through the door.
Upon check-in, there was no sign of cleaning in the room, except for the bed being made. There was hair everywhere (definitely not ours), dust, some lint, etc. The black grout in the bathroom also suggests that this was not a one-time problem. Additionally, room service is available for an additional fee, what about longer stays?
The lack of amenities also resulted in the scarcity of soap and bath liquid (in other words, the lack of any disposable cosmetics, which are standard), just 1.5 rolls of cheap and short toilet paper.
In the bathroom, the lighting was also partially not working and there was no option to turn off the terribly loud fan. The tap in the sink only worked with a motion sensor and this was rather inconvenient and generally seems like an absurd choice in a hotel.
The bed was not very comfortable, low and terribly small. My feet were sticking out from under it and the width was more like a large one, not a small two.
They even skimped on a second desk chair or an ice tray for the freezer.
The appearance, of course, depends on taste, but we didn't like it. So unkempt, it looked like some old student dormitory.
Overall I don't recommend it, but...

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The location is good, about 5 minutes to the center.
neg_text:
We did not receive the room booked as described, but instead a large, accessible room for people with disabilities. Everything is outdated and not furnished in a modern style.

The breakfast was okay, but it was the same thing every day or every other day, which was a shame.
Mattresses very soft.
Just FYI, no German TV, but that didn't bother us.
In conclusion, it's okay for sleeping for one or two nights.

title: Pleasant
pos_text: The breakfast was plentiful and good, lots of different food options, lovely bread
neg_text: The room and bathroom smelled horrible.

title: Good for a few days; actually has the potential for more.
pos_text:
- central location, parking, beds, hygiene
- Breakfast room
neg_text: - The TV remote was defective and was supposed to be replaced, but I received no further response. - The breakfast selection could have been better, but it was okay, and the service was friendly.

title: Business Wroclaw
pos_text: Good breakfasts, quite a large selection.
neg_text: The beds were uncomfortable, I had the impression that they were not very clean

title: We stayed here for 9 days…. 9 days to long. Fortunately the beautiful City made up for the disappointing accommodation
pos_text: Location was good
neg_text: When booking the hotel, the room selected was nothing like the room we were given. Very disappointing! Dated bathroom. Serviced after a 5 day stay. I would compare our room to a UK travel lodge.

title: It's much nicer in Wrocław!
pos_text: The location is great!
neg_text:
It serves its purpose but lacks a feel-good factor and has several drawbacks!
We were allowed to change rooms because the first one smelled very rotten from the siphon. The rooms generally have the charm of a hospital room (everything is wheelchair accessible) - but they do NOT correspond to the photos on Booking.com. No housekeeping during your stay!
Dust was constantly falling from the ventilation system above the bed. The toilet brush had already seen better days. The refrigerator only works when you are in the room! Few electrical outlets and no bedside table for the person sleeping outside. TV only had Polish channels - unfortunately, battery acid leaked from the remote control!
There is a massive rat infestation in the neighboring building (rear entrance to the hotel and parking lot)!

title: You shouldn't expect much from this hotel.

Location close to the city center.
Quite a large room.
Delicious varied breakfast
neg_text: Room without carpet, old hair dryer, no parking spaces

title: Pictures and amenities of the room do not meet reality
pos_text: Close to the city centre
neg_text:
No heating
Sheets seemed used not ironed
Receptionist rather indifferent not willing to solve the problem.

title: Did not meet our expectations
pos_text: The partition for the 2nd bedroom
neg_text: No kitchenette, photos are not contractual. During the day in the rooms at sunrise, the view overlooks a disused patio.

title: Pleasant
neg_text: The bed was quite narrow and against the wall. The room was very minimalist. There is a strange smell in the room, like cigarettes.

title: Passable
pos_text: the room was clean, the staff was really nice and polite, overall the hotel was quiet, and the location was comfortable
neg_text: They gave me room for disabled people. There's nothing bad about that, but because of the special purpose of this room, there was no carpet and no slippers to wear inside of the room. Also, I don't understand why there's no toothpaste. I am aware that the hotel has 3 stars, but at least toothpaste must have been there.

title: Suitable for short stays due to location and price, with compromises in comfort and flair.
pos_text: Good location, spacious rooms, ample and good breakfast, reasonably priced rooms
neg_text:
The rooms felt hospital-like, probably due to the enormous ceiling height, the smell of disinfectant, and the accessible bathroom with its corresponding equipment. The latter is not a bad thing in itself, however, apparently all rooms are like that except for a few that are pictured on the portal. However, it was not clear to us which category they belonged to when we selected our rooms. Our booked king-size bed turned out to be two single beds pushed together with hard mattresses and rustling bedwetting protectors…
Considering the room price, I found the parking fee of €15 per night to be relatively high.

title: Location, expensive.
pos_text: Location
neg_text: Dirty bedspread, no parking included in the price, even though I asked about it during the reservation...

title: Don’t believe the photos!
pos_text: Great location. Good price.
neg_text: The photos advertising the rooms DO NOT match at all. My room looked like a prison with one sad over used pillow and orange and green tiles in the bathroom. The windows were small and so high up I felt like I was in a cell. When I complained, I got a different room, bigger, more acceptable, but still nowhere near the standard advertised.

title: Great place if you do a lot of sightseeing - rather poor rest.
pos_text: Friendly and efficient service. Great location :)
neg_text: The rooms are not soundproofed - you can hear absolutely everything, from the neighbor's hairdryer to conversations in the corridor and slamming doors. The photos differ from what is actually on site. Very soft and uncomfortable bed. What's worse - during the night a man knocked on our door four times, and from what we could hear, it was not only us who was doing it. The lady at the reception knew nothing about the situation. It's a bit strange that the facility's security didn't react because it was around 3/4 am and the man was being loud.

title: Passable
neg_text: Staff was not nice and we have two separate beds in our room even we are a couple. Room decoration was awful there is a huge desk in the room from wall to wall

title: Wrocław is very nice and very beautiful.
pos_text: Good breakfast, good location and quiet. Spacious room, but with two very small beds (90 cm)
neg_text:
Upon opening the top of the cupboard, we found a bag with food scraps, which was very unpleasant.
We took it to reception and the girl didn't offer much of an apology either. We also didn't know that if you want them to clean the room, you have to pay extra. We had a car and the garage they provided was a bit difficult to access and the girl didn't explain it very well; in addition to paying for it separately (which we already knew and wasn't a problem), they kept 160 Zl for the remote control since the space was in the garage of a residential building.

title: The first and last time
pos_text: Fairly good breakfast, good location.
neg_text: At the entrance we were greeted by the stench of burning cigarettes, and the sheets on one of the beds were dirty. There was a used condom on the windowsill. After informing the ladies at the reception about the above, there was no reaction, only a request to send a photo to the provided e-mail address. After writing a complaint by email, no response. I will not book again.

title: Passable

neg_text: The sockets didn't work, there was no light in the bathroom, the TV didn't work

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
Gegend war gut. Unschlagbar. In 5 Minuten biste in der City. Saubere Zimmer, allerdings ist die Bequemlichkeit relativ gering.
In der Stadt gibt es sehr viele Möglichkeiten für Cafés oder Kneipen. Tipp: die Cuba Bar am Rynek. Leckere Cocktails. Aber auch hier: kaum Kundenorientiertheit. Sehr unfreundlich und alle nur am Handy. Man muss lange warten bis mal Jemand kommt. Grundsätzlich ist der Dienstleistungssektor gerade in Cafés und Restaurants sehr schlecht. Auch in den Zabkas viele unfreundliche Menschen die nicht so richtig Bock auf ihre Arbeit haben und es am Kunden auslassen. Hier also Vorsicht!.
neg_text: Fenster lassen sich nicht öffnen. Keine Bar oder Kühlschrank unten in der Lobby wo man sich etwas kaufen kann. Frühstück für polnische Verhältnisse sehr teuer und nichts besonderes. Einige der weiblichen Angestellten sehr arrogant und wenig freundlich. Andere hingegen sehr Kundenorientiert und freundlich, zuvorkommend.

title: Ein Aufenthalt mit Enttäuschung, in einer Weltstadt.
pos_text: Die Lage ist gut! Breslau ist eine Reise wert. Wir kamen aus Krakau für ein paar Stunden nach Breslau. Ich konnte das Zimmer online buchen und online bezahlen (eine andere Unterkunft in Breslau wollte das Geld nur per Bank überwiesen haben, was allerdings an demselben Tag nicht möglich war. Eine Bezahlung mit Kreditkarte vor Ort war auch nicht möglich). Daher waren wir froh, dass wir in diesem Hotel so schnell und freundlich von Krakau aus bedient wurden! Vielen Dank!
neg_text: Die Enttäuschung kam aber vor Ort. Im Internet sehen die Zimmer sehr gut aus, aber unsere Ausstattung war ziemlich schlecht (ein altes Bett, und leider kein Ehebett, wie wir es vorbestellt hatten). Hinzu kam noch, dass wir für das Aufräumen des Zimmers extra zahlen sollten (25 zl = 5 €). Das hatten wir bis jetzt in keiner Unterkunft in der Welt, v.a. wenn man vorher alles normal gebucht hat.

title: Passable

Bardzo ciężki kontakt telefoniczny.

title: powrót nieciekawy - broni się, trochę, lokalizacją
pos_text: Lokalizacja, lokalizacja, lokalizacja, bardzo blisko Starego Wrocławia i wszystkich jego atrakcji i restauracji
neg_text:
Cóż, ostatni raz byłem w hotelu przed zmiana właściciela, potem był zamknięty myślałem, ze remont - super.
Niestety jest gorzej nic nie wyremontowane, a wręcz przeciwnie całość na chwilę obecna "trąci już myszka". Wymienione zostały natomiast łóżka na gorsze, dużo niższe i dużo twardsze, wręcz niewygodne. Toalety duże ale już archaiczne, w TV dwa kanały po polsku ale za to 300.. po arabsku i kilkanaście po włosku - wiadomo hotbird. Przy rezerwacji nie było informacji, że trzeba rezerwować miejsce parkingowe piszą wiadomość do hotelu - nie ma czasu na takie procedury. Kiedyś było funkcjonalne patio z zadaszonymi fotelami i stolikami teraz parking samochodów dostawczych (podobno służbowych) przykro.

The heating in the room didn't work, and I didn't really like the fact that the room had a wooden floor and not carpet.
When I checked into the room, the TV and bathroom light were not working. The maintenance man quickly repaired the fault, but the bad taste remained.

title: Good location, nice facade, and that's about it.
pos_text: The location was great; we could reach all destinations on foot.
neg_text: Behind the beautiful facade lies a boring hotel with small, tasteless rooms. Extremely simple and definitely not recommended.

title: Pleasant

neg_text: The air conditioning wasn't working, the breakfast is too expensive for what it offers, and the staff aren't exactly friendly.

title: Good hotel with a booking problem
pos_text:
The city center with the Christmas market is easily accessible via a 5-minute walk.
The hotel has its own parking lot in the courtyard, with approximately 20 parking spaces. You have to reserve in advance. Otherwise, you have to drive into a parking garage. A deposit must be paid for the barrier key.
The breakfast buffet is VERY extensive and offers a selection ranging from toppings to fish. There's something for everyone.
neg_text:
The booking process is a bit strange. In retrospect, I can no longer understand it. Rooms with private bathrooms are available at relatively low prices. When making a reservation, you are informed that you can also book with a bathroom, for an additional charge. Okay, surprised, but they do it. You get the new price. An even higher price will then be debited from the account!?!?
The young ladies at reception are completely overwhelmed by the booking computer... Temporary workers..?
The information provided is that the high final price resulted from the currency conversion, but €7?

title: Pleasant
pos_text: I got an accessibility room and it was huge. Lots of space and storage- could have been a studio flat.
neg_text:
Bed was ok.
Comforter wasn’t laid out - it was underneath the pillow so I almost didn’t find it.

title: In terms of quality-price ratio, the price is high.
pos_text:
The breakfast was excellent and there was a wide range of options.
Great location - close to the historic center
neg_text:
I was at the hotel about five years ago and I was thrilled. Unfortunately, in 2023, everything remained original, so the bed was worn out, the sheets were washed (it was clear that they were originally white - now gray), the sheets were not ironed and, moreover, they were covered with plastic - like in a crib for small children. So it rustles underneath you all night :(. Dust piles under the bed, dust on the shelves.
The room was very noisy - both street noise and noise from the room next door.
What surprised me was the lack of parking. Even though I reserved a parking space, the reception told me that the entire hotel parking lot was occupied. So we had to look for a place nearby and of course pay a higher amount for city parking. However, the hotel parking lot was empty all night and the following morning - we had a view of the parking lot from our room window.

title: Passable

title: The city is beautiful, the hotel is centrally located, but apart from the breakfast, the service staff is very unfriendly.
pos_text: The breakfast was very good and plentiful. Unfortunately, the staff doesn't speak German and gets annoyed 😒 if they don't immediately understand everything in English. Inquiries about the parking lot will not be answered; no information will be provided. No service for guests from abroad.
neg_text: Booking the hotel room is annoying; we received about 10 emails stating that bookings could not be made. The booked price was not honored. Approximately 30 euros more were debited.

neg_text: For several days no one came to clean the room. And the worst thing is that the room looks like a hospital room (especially the bathroom). This makes me feel very uneasy. I feel very uncomfortable because of this.

pos_text: The breakfast even if it is paid and the location.
neg_text: A hotel that gives you a room for 4 people but there's a sofa bed that you have to make yourself is not a hotel but an apartment. Parking in the garage is not paid per night but from 12 to 12 (never seen anything like this in all my years of travelling),

title: Disappointed
pos_text: The location is definitely a plus. Friendly and helpful service. Parking on site.
neg_text: Unfortunately, upon entering the room, we were greeted by an exceptionally strong and unpleasant sewer smell coming from the bathroom. We reported the problem and the hotel staff helped as much as they could. We were ready to change the room to another one, even smaller/cheaper, but that day there was a boxing gala in Wrocław and the hotel was fully booked. If the hotel reads this review, I would like to suggest that you replace the blinds in the master bedroom (we were in a 4-person classic room on the top floor). - unfortunately, you probably had an air conditioning failure and there is a large stain from the leak on the roller shutter under the air conditioning unit, running along its entire length. The price-quality ratio, especially with the sewer smell, does not give the feeling of money well spent. Ps. The hotel also had a problem with their booking account being hacked. A hacker pretending to be booking from the Dikul hotel account tried to trick us into providing our card number and PIN; I feel sorry for the hotel but I assume the problem was most likely due to a weak password to the hotel account.

title: It could have been better.
pos_text: The location is very good, close to the center. The service was very nice.
neg_text: I think that for a 3-star hotel, the decor and appearance of this place should be better. Even a 2-star hotel in Katowice was better furnished.

title: I don't recommend it.
pos_text: The location is very good.
neg_text: Average room, age and wear of the equipment are visible. Average cleanliness. And the worst thing: unfortunately, the air conditioning in the room did not work. My stay occurred on a hot day and a very hot night. The next day, early in the morning, I had things to do. In the evening, after returning to the room, it turned out that the air conditioning was not working. I asked the lady at the reception for help. She couldn't turn on the air conditioning and didn't offer to change rooms. She did, however, state that she could send a maintenance technician (at 00:30), but it was not known how long the repair would take and, understandably, there was no guarantee that the repair would be successful. In the end it was a sleepless night. Stuffy, very hot. The windows could only be slightly opened, but then the noise from the street made it impossible to sleep. Overall a failure. When I asked for a discount the next day, a late check-out (to come back after taking care of things) or free breakfast - I was told that this was not possible because I had already paid and there was no manager on site. I was offered a late check-out of 30 minutes! In a word, I was blown away. The ladies at the reception were rude and unhelpful. I'm not one of those demanding and argumentative people, but here I was treated condescendingly and rudely.

Our daughter stayed for free, but we only had two made beds.
An annoying sensor-activated faucet in the bathroom, just like in public restrooms. Two lamps by the bed without power, as there are no power outlets anywhere. A blackout blind that rattles loudly in the slightest breeze. Wallpaper that is peeling off the wall. A wobbly shower tray. Etc.
The building is cool, but the hotel inside seems out of place.

title: Takže sem už nikdy.
pos_text: Jediné, co se nám líbilo bylo umístění hotelu, blízko do centra a práce slečen na recepci.
neg_text: Při příjezdu nám řekli, že pro nás bohužel nemají parkovací místo, ale nabídli nám náhradní v podzemní garáži asi 200m., ale musíte dát zálohu 150 zlotých, které musíte zaplatit v hotovosti, kterou jsem bohužel neměl. Tak jsme si zaplatili hodinu parkovného u hotelu a šli hledat bankomat. Když jsme se vrátili, dostali jsme kartu od pokoje a jeli se do 5.patra ubytovat. Když jsme zkusili odemknout pokoj,karta bohužel nefungovala, takže zase dolů na recepci, kde mi slečna s úsměvem kartu přeprogramovala a pro jistotu dala ještě kartu druhou. Po opětovném vyjetí do 5.patra jsem opět zjistil, že ani jedna karta nefunguje. Vrátil jsem se na recepci a sdělil slečně, ať to vyřeší a já jdu zaparkovat vůz. Slečna mi popsala cestu a já vyrazil. Cestou jsem na radu recepční projel 2x v protisměru jednosměrky ( prý je to tu normální ) a přijel k bráně, kde kupodivu můj dálkový ovladač opět nefungoval. Takže jsem opět zpět na recepci, kde se mě ujímá jiná slečna a jede se mnou do garáže. Ovšem do jiné, než mě poslala slečna první. Ok. Po zaparkování ( měl jsem číslo 95, které po příjezdu samozřejmě bylo obsazené) jsem opět na recepci a s příslibem, že je vše opraveno se vracím do pokoje, kde už byla manželka. Když jsem vešel do pokoje, zjistil jsem, že absolutně neodpovídá tomu, co jsme si objednali. Ptám se ženy, kde jsou karty, že zajdu na recepci, prý je odnesl opravář. Jsem opět na recepci, kde si stěžuji, že jsme dostali jiný pokoj. Slečna mě ujišťovala, že pokoj máme správný a že na fotce jsou jen jiné barvy. Objednali jsme si pokoj s manželskou postelí KING. Ta naše na pokoji neměla na šířku ani 160 cm. Ona říkala, že všechny pokoje mají tuto postel. Když jsem se zeptal, jetli všechny koupelny jsou pro invalidy, tak řekla, že ne a přestala se usmívat. Po upozornění, že jsme si určitě pokoj pro invalidu neobjednal, řekla, že jiný pokoj nemají a že si můžu stěžovat přes email. Dobře, snad to tu 2 noci nějak vydržíme. Unfortunately, after entering the room, consternation and surprise because the factual situation and photos are two different stories. The room was dark, as if it had come straight from the communist era, and the bathroom looked like a hospital bathroom from the communist era, the only thing missing was a white stool on metal legs. The nice lady at the reception told me that unfortunately all the rooms look like this, including the apartment offered. Yes, I think the photos were generated by AI or the owner's wishful thinking. I know there is competition on the market, etc., but I believe that honesty towards customers comes first. Otherwise, it resembles a sanatorium, or perhaps it was a retirement home.
The bed is so deep, it almost feels like sleeping on an air mattress. The room is large, so why are the beds so small? Only 1.40 meters wide!

Unfortunately, the bathroom is not a place where one feels comfortable at all. It's all age-appropriate, which is certainly helpful for some guests, but it has nothing to do with a nice bathroom in a hotel. The smell is unbearable and the towels are old, worn out and smell very unpleasant.

The elevator or the door is so extremely loud when opening that you can hear it all along the hallway.

The breakfast is good, but there is only one coffee machine, which also takes a very long time. Why not put up at least two? The breakfast room is small and too crowded; not everyone can find a seat. Unfortunately, it's also very uncomfortable.

All in all, the stay was unfortunately not relaxing. It was supposed to be a short break. The price is comparatively cheaper, but by no means justified. Half would have been more appropriate.

For us, this was our first and last stay there.

title: The hotel fell short of expectations and was very expensive for what it offered.
pos_text: Good location for visiting the center but not ideal if you want to be near the bus or train station. Spacious room and presence of a small fridge.
neg_text: Cleanliness leaves a little to be desired (the sheets were not clean and the table had some dust on it). Some of the reception staff were smiling, while others seemed quite annoyed by our presence (so unkind that they didn't even explain or give us the note with the Wi-Fi password on it). But the worst thing was the bathroom. We reported that the sink handle was spinning almost 360 degrees and needed to be repaired, and it was also impossible to take a shower without flooding the bathroom. After just 2 minutes, water was pouring out of the shower (you can see from the photos that it's actually a walk-in shower, so the slope isn't right, it floods everything) and it was impossible to keep the water running for even 5 minutes. Hostel-like sheets, small and uncomfortable pillows, and there were no bathroom products (shampoo or shower gel). I think paying almost 150 euros a night to then have these terrible services is unacceptable. If you find a room for 60 euros a night, that's fine...but if not, avoid it and go somewhere else. I wouldn't go back.

On the day of check-in, we arrived at the hotel around 6 p.m. After receiving the keys and opening the room, we were immediately hit by the stench of the bathroom sewage system. We reported this fact immediately to the hotel reception but unfortunately all we heard was that there was no one to take care of it and that we would not be given another room. We were told that we could only write a complaint on the hotel form, which of course we did. I also asked for a refund, we wanted to stay overnight in another hotel but I was also told there was no possibility of receiving a refund.
To date, and some time has passed, we have not received a response to the complaint, and I would also like to mention that I have not received an invoice for the service (I reported this when booking the hotel). It's a shame that hotel guests are being ignored like this, because we would probably use the services of this hotel more than once, and after this situation we definitely won't do so and we advise others not to do so.

After I asked the reception why I didn't get the room I paid for (approx. PLN 350 per night) The lady initially said that they no longer had these rooms available, but after a while she changed her mind and said that the photos were only illustrative. I wonder why the hotel doesn't put real photos on the booking page instead of illustrative ones, which are inaccurate anyway?

It is also worth remembering that cleaning is paid - PLN 25. The hotel does not include cleaning in the price. I didn't find this information on booking, but I read it at the reception desk.

I definitely do not recommend it - a huge disappointment and a waste of money.

The owner defended himself by claiming that these were “illustrative photos” and there may be a difference, but for me it is a scam and misleading guests. I always choose my hotel very carefully and this is the first time I have had such a terrible experience. The bed has a torn bottom, the pillows have been washed a million times so much that the filling is made of compacted oakum and is impossible to sleep on, the towels are gray, hard, and frayed, there are no bedside lamps or sockets by the beds, and the iron is burnt out. Service - on the plus side I can add that at least they found me another room where the bed wasn't torn. But he was even uglier.
The only positive thing, after the intervention of the booking.com customer service office: after filing a complaint, I got some of my money back. However, what I ended up paying still absolutely does not match the standard I slept in. Three stars is a misunderstanding.

I wholeheartedly recommend this hotel if you don't want to be disappointed like we were. We arrived exhausted after traveling halfway across the country to the Christmas market. We had specifically booked a king room to have more space, but what we found there left us stunned. The photos presented on the website have no relation to reality, it's not about a different furniture arrangement or a differently made bed - THE ENTIRE ROOM WAS 3 CLASSES BELOW WHAT WE SIGNED FOR. We were greeted by an unpleasant smell, the entire space was filled with rickety furniture, and on the bed were two unmade duvets covered in dust and HAIR! There was also hair all over the scratched floor. The string of the large roller blind was broken, so the only thing covering the windows were dirty curtains. The walls were scratched and scuffed, and in the bathroom we were greeted by cobwebs and a spider on them.

The longer we stayed there, the more we felt cheated and finally we decided not to stay in this hotel any longer, so we reported it to Booking and moved to the JAZZ hotel, 4 minutes away, where we actually felt like we were in a hotel, and the illustrative photos are not misleading.

THIS IS DEFINITELY NOT A 200 PLN PER NIGHT HOTEL, AT LEAST 50 PLN.

I will just add that the only thing we have recovered so far is PLN 60 and the photos showing the King room on the hotel booking page are photos of a 4-person room. Below I am attaching the actual appearance of the King room of the "hotel" dikul ;)

title: Given a small room we did not book, refused an upgrade or compensation, terrible customer service!!
pos_text: The only saving grace was this room appeared clean.
neg_text:
My partner and I decided to treat ourselves and booked one of the larger suites at Hotel Dikul. However, upon arrival we were checked into an small double room just off reception on the ground floor. It was clearly a room
for people with accessibility issues. The room was dated and, compared to the suite we booked, was considerably smaller. It looked nothing like the suite advertised on booking.com’s site. I checked my booking to confirm that I had in fact booked the other room, and I had. We went to reception to request a room move. We were told there were no other rooms. I asked to speak with the manger… they weren’t around. The person on reception looked very young and was extremely unhelpful. I was told that was the room I had booked, which it clearly wasn’t. There was no acknowledgment or apology whatsoever. I asked for a refund if I wasn’t able to stay in the room I had booked. All I was given was a complaints form to hand write. Thankfully our stay was only for 1 night. Upon checkout we were asked if we enjoyed our stay. We explained the situation to the new receptionist who proceeded to sit silently and stare at us. An apology really would have gone a long way. All I can think and hope is that they aren’t used to people not enjoying their stay, which may be reassuring to others I guess. For us the customer service was truly terrible. A week or so after our stay I was emailed a discount offer for my next booking at hotel dikul!! Needless to say I would never ever stay there again. Also to note that the hotel isn’t actually on the market square. It’s close to the market
Square and is still in a convenient location, but we felt the name on here is a little misleading.

title: Péssima experiência, não recomendo a ninguém.
pos_text: Nada.
neg_text:
Fizemos o check-in pelas 20 horas, foi nos dado o quarto 305, deixamos os nossos pertences no mesmo e fomos passear pela cidade. Apenas quando regressámos, pelas 00:00 horas, e dirigiamo-nos para dormir, deparamo-nos que a cama encontrava-se partida em várias zonas, com diversas tábuas de madeira fora da sua posição e os plásticos de suporte às mesmas partidos. Posto isto, alertamos de imediato a recepcionista que se encontrava de serviço a essa hora (não foi a mesma que realizou o nosso check-in) e tiramos fotos para comprovar o mesmo. A recepcionista veio então ao nosso quarto e confirmou o estado da mesma. Posto isto, pedimos então que nos fosse apresentada uma alternativa (mudar de quarto ou então substituírem a cama), o que nos foi dada uma resposta negativa, pois segundo a recepcionista o hotel encontrava-se lotado e não havia mais nenhum quarto livre, sendo a única alternativa reembolsar o dinheiro. Como é obvio, demonstramos o nosso descontentamento, pois a uma hora tão tardia não era conveniente trocar de hotel e pedimos o formulário para fazer a reclamação, este que foi lido e assinado pela recepcionista.
Tivemos então infelizmente de pernoitar nesse quarto com a cama partida, e na manhã do dia seguinte abandonamos o hotel. A recepcionista que se encontrava de serviço era mesma que realizou o nosso check-in do dia anterior, e então decidimos informa-lá também da situação, ao que ela respondeu que supostamente seriamos contactados pelo hotel (?), e se caso não fossemos, forneceu-nos um email para que possamos enviar a nossa reclamação.
Para que não fosse suficiente, o quarto encontrava-se também com um nível de limpeza horrível, cheio de teias de aranha nas paredes e no teto e um cheiro nauseabundo (uma espécie de cheiro a peido/esgoto) vindo da casa de banho, a agua do chuveiro não tinha pressão e um barulho incomodativo (uma espécie de objetos a cair etc…) no corredor logo pela manhã do dia seguinte.

2 hours before arriving at the venue, the guest made a phone call to inquire about parking - the guest was informed that parking was available on site behind the barrier, the guest only needed to find a parking space for a moment, pay for parking at the reception and then enter the parking lot. Upon arrival, it was impossible to find any temporary place, despite driving around the entire area 3 times. The receptionist advised us to park the car in front of the "emergency" entrance for a while. The guest went inside, wanted to pay, and was informed that he did not have a parking reservation, so he could not park there. I would like to emphasize that the hotel description included information that reserving a parking space was not possible due to the small number of spaces, but the hotel directs guests to nearby parking lots. The receptionist nonchalantly said that they didn't direct you to any parking lots, so you could look for a public space around the hotel. As the icing on the cake - we sent a request for a cake with a card with wishes to be delivered to the room (it was the birthday of a hotel guest), the receptionist did not include a birthday card despite previous assurances, and brought the cake to the room with the receipt attached to it. We are embarrassed by the level of service and will be requesting a refund - the guest did not stay overnight at the hotel after such an unprofessional welcome.

title: Bad
pos_text: The worst hotel in terms of price/quality ratio. I will never come here again and I do not advise you to choose this hotel. We booked a room with a king size bed. This was indicated as an advantage when booking, there are screenshots. Also, when booking, it is indicated that there is parking. After booking a room, I wrote that I would like to reserve paid parking on the hotel grounds, but I was refused “there are no spaces.” However, after checking into my room at 11:00 pm, I saw that there were a lot of free spaces in the hotel parking lot. I had to look for parking myself; there is a paid city parking lot near the hotel, but finding a free space there turned out to be very difficult. Instead of a room with a large bed, we were given a room for people with disabilities. The bed was low on the floor, without legs, and its width was 140 cm!!!! You can only approach it from one side! The mattress is very soft and uncomfortable, it is impossible to understand what it is made of, this is the first time I have encountered this in a hotel. She stands close to the wall. For 2 people in the room there are only 3 towels that are not the freshest. The table was placed close to the window, which made it impossible to open/close the window and ventilate the room. There is a kettle, but water is not provided. The air conditioner was on very cold air, right above the bed, blowing in your face, it was impossible to turn it off, the remote control didn’t work, attempts to change the batteries on my own didn’t help, probably the remote control was wrong. The refrigerator didn't work. The TV was no longer turned on. The sink and toilet in the bathroom are also for people with disabilities, as is the door (it doesn't close). The door in the shower stall is broken: It didn't open or close, you had to crawl through a small crack. In the evening, the hall on the 2nd floor smelled strongly of feces. At 3 and 5 o'clock in the morning there was a lot of noise in the corridor. After we saw our room, we went to the reception, the girl said that she would not change the room, and that all their rooms are like this, and what is listed on booking and booked means nothing!!!!
neg_text: I didn't like everything

title: The guest is worthless here! Once paid, nothing matters!
