neg_text: First of all, it must be said that the pictures have nothing to do with reality. I am writing here an objective review of my experiences that night with this house. The staff is extremely unfriendly and unprofessional, to the point where you can say you are at a loss for words. My air conditioning in my room didn't work. I complained about this and the lady said she couldn't give me another room because I had already been in this room for two hours. Okay man, I understand the world. Because normally if an electrical device doesn't work you are given another room. She confirmed to me Rooms would be available. After more than 4 hours, a man came and looked at the air conditioning and also found that it was broken. The room was not changed here. You could say the staff just didn't care. Basically not a problem but that day it was very hot. They leave the guest out in the cold and refuse to give me another room even though I was confirmed that rooms were available. also. The equipment has nothing to do with the description. Mattresses are rock hard. The street is extremely noisy. Parking is available if you're lucky, otherwise you'll have a long search for a parking space. I have never met such bad staff as when a device in the room is broken and the guest is not given another room with the justification that I was in the room for more than 2 hours, even though I hadn't used anything. that's a disgrace. The building is very noisy. The award at Booking.com 9.2 is fair￼ The only thing one could actually say is that the breakfast is okay for the price! Once and never again and things like that, I rarely say.

title: Pleasant
pos_text:
The rooms are very nice and clean, which is a huge plus for the entire facility. The room has a safe and toiletries needed for traveling, as well as a refrigerator. The ladies at the reception and in the restaurant are very nice and helpful, very polite.
Very tasty meals in the afternoon buffet open from 4 p.m., we took advantage of it as a family - very large portions, tasty and relatively inexpensive.
neg_text:
Even though the facility is presented as a hotel and recreation/sports center, there is no gym and a PAID swimming pool also for hotel guests, PLN 24 for an adult and an additional PLN 12 for a swimming cap if you do not have one. The lady at the reception desk at the pool wasn't very nice, but I understand that someone can have a bad day.
Breakfasts rated as fantastic leave a lot to be desired. Since the breakfast restaurant was open until 11 a.m. on the weekend, one would expect that items would still be added to the buffet at 9 a.m. Unfortunately, nothing could be further from the truth, at 9:00 one inedible cold cut.
Some of the products were very tasty and fresh, but the buffet also included dried fruit and nuts in glass jars, as well as homemade jams and preserves, which looked very tempting - so my husband and I decided to take advantage of these benefits. Unfortunately, in nuts and dried fruit - the next day we found mold, which is an absolute disqualification for me.

The hotel is ok as a stopover, but the most disappointing thing is the breakfast, which is relatively expensive - for this price you can find a really good quality breakfast place in Wrocław, and in the city center.

It was extremely hot and hard to breathe, as the air was musty and not fresh.
Room didn't have AC, which is acceptable. The real problem - it didn't have proper ventilation. If you open a window - a lot of bugs and mosquitos (remember, hotel is in green area, next to river) and fresh air will not really come due to ventilation problems. So even if you ask for more expensive room with air conditioning - it will not fix the problem.

When we arrived, there were a bunch of mosquitos and other bugs in the room (on photo). Bedsheets had some stains. Beds are terrible - creaking and uncomfortable.

You are always charged for parking for 24hours, even though it is shared with other premises and has actually hourly pricing.

We were not lucky - hotel hosted big group of teens coming for some sport competition I guess. So it was loud,no benefit of "quite green area".
Disadvantage of green area is present - it's far from everything, if you'd like to switch from car to public transport - bad idea.

In summary: pay a little more and have a comfortable stay somewhere else

The restaurant is unfortunately a mistake. The waiter has no idea what he's selling. The dishes on the menu are burnt and overcooked. Old cakes. Prices are ok and positive the fact that after such culinary experiences there were no stomach revolutions. We weren't tempted to eat breakfast anymore. I regret that I was guided by the restaurant's reviews, which are rated 5/5 on the internet - but as it turned out, poor reviews are not published :) Sad…

title: I regret every penny spent on vacation in this place.
neg_text:
The most embarrassing vacation I've ever had. When booking your stay through Booking.com, there is no information about even a paid swimming pool, jacuzzi and sauna for hotel guests, the pool is filled with children and there are only two lanes left for guests, the jacuzzi does not work, and there is only one sauna and, moreover, it does not look like in the photos.
Regarding the photos posted by the hotel on booking.com, don't be fooled by the beautiful saunas, relaxation areas and other beautiful photos they posted because you won't be able to use them as a guest anyway, unless you delve deeper into the topic or you are made aware at the hotel that everything that is renovated and beautiful is part of some kind of wellness program, which also comes at an extra cost. So, when we arrived at the hotel to relax in the pool, jacuzzi and sauna and paid PLN 300 for the room, we had to pay an additional PLN 60 for an hour of sauna, which is hard to relax in. We didn't even use the pool, for which we received a voucher because we argued with the staff and were cheated, because they scared us away from the pool because of the children, of whom there were a lot, and the out-of-order jacuzzi. I wouldn't be so upset about it if I didn't know that it's often cheaper in hotels (sometimes even almost half the price) Amenities such as the swimming pool and sauna are free and unlimited. You don't have to ask for anything, we even found bathrobes and extra towels in the rooms, and no one showed anything in the ad that wasn't actually there, or rather, there is, but you have to book and pay in advance. I regret that I booked this hotel because I had in mind another one, proven, cheaper and with full access to wellness. I will never set foot here again, and I will never say a good word about it.
The hotel staff, as in other reviews, were very rude and did nothing to address our concerns before and after arrival.

I also didnt like the extra stuffed animals in the room. My children travel with their own stuffed animals. And stuffed animals are notorious for being bug infested. I dont want my kids cuddling up to some stuffed animal whose history I dont know. Its kind of disgusting to be honest. BUT: we really loved the little princess castle in the room. title: Bad experience
neg_text:
We noticed that the central heating wasn't working. I called the reception to inform them. They sent a man to fix it. He informed us that it started working properly now, but that we shouldn't have it on full speed because it gives cold air (????) and he set it on low speed. Half an hour later the air coming from the a/c was cold. I called again the reception to let them know, and the girl of the reception told me that her colleague informed her that it will take some time and we should wait 15-30 minutes. Half an hour later I went down to the reception, asked the girl to go and check it on her self that the air coming it's cold. She insisted that she just spoke again with her colleague which informed her that the room's temperature just started increasing and it will be warmer and warmer.
After we spent a couple of hours in the room and waited the temperature to get warmer, we asked them to change us the room. They said that they don't have another room and they brought us a small fan heater. The bathroom was cold throughout the whole night, the fan heater was very very noise and we had to close it too at night, leaving the room very cold.
The worst part is that I informed them that this is going to be a bad review for their hotel and they couldn't care less. They lied from the beginning as they knew that their heating is having issues in all the building (I have read some reviews afterwards from other customers), they made us spend 2 hours inside the room to wait for the heating to start, even though we only had 1 day to see the city.
At the end we preferred to sleep in a cold room without the noise of the fan heater.
I do not recommend this hotel to anyone. Totally unprofessional people. The worst 150€ we spent on a single night.

pos_text: We were just passing through Wroclaw and needed somewhere to stay the night. I would say this place is decent for that reason. neg_text: We stayed during Saturday and the hotel had warned us that there will be a wedding. When we arrived, hotel staff gave us the room on the fourth floor with a nice medium sized window overlooking Wroclaw. neg_text: When checking in, I was not informed that there was a party going on that night. After 10 p.m. I called the reception to ask why it was so loud since the night time silence is from 10 p.m. and then I was informed that there was a birthday party. Unfortunately, loud music and noise until 2 a.m. did not allow for a peaceful night.

title: Not for me then!
pos_text: Localities. Not far from the center :-), parking in front of the hotel.
neg_text: The bathroom in the room does not match the photos here on booking.com. The bathroom was a big disappointment. During our stay, there were major sporting events taking place at the hotel. For this reason, it was not possible to use the hotel restaurant, this fact was concealed from us upon arrival and we were told that the restaurant was open. The same problem occurred at breakfast, when only one table was allocated to the remaining hotel guests, and they didn't even have time to keep it clean. Breakfast was a big disappointment. We were not notified of the fact that the hotel was hosting such a large event, even through hotel communication on booking before arrival. Furthermore, the room was missing a mini fridge. It probably wasn't included, but assuming the hotel restaurant couldn't be used, it was a necessity for cooling drinks. Furthermore, the sound insulation from the surrounding rooms and corridor is poorly addressed. From my point of view, very unmanageable and uncomfortable. I would not stay here again.

title: Not intended for couples or relaxing weekend trips.
pos_text: The proximity to the train station was advantageous – it takes about a quarter of an hour to get to the old town.
neg_text:
Events were announced via booking (after booking, mind you). I assume it's a bit louder inside the building itself, but not that the bass is thumping into the room until 1 a.m. so much that the bed vibrates. And quite honestly, I'm a good sleeper and very understanding, but... Such noise levels are unacceptable, especially given the hotel's policy of quiet hours from 10 pm to 6 am. After I asked for a discount, I was turned away rudely - according to the motto: I would have received an email and could have cancelled. My hairdryer in the room didn't work, neither did the access card to the room on Saturday, and I had to wait three-quarters of an hour at 10 pm to get into my room (no compensation was offered here either). The receptionists seemed more overwhelmed and annoyed than solution-oriented towards the guests. Keep your eyes open when choosing a job - I say this as a former hotel manager in what was then Berlin Mitte. I must commend the cleaning service. The lady was very helpful and also very friendly.

I cannot comment on breakfast and the restaurant - the bar was closed due to the parties. I hadn't booked breakfast - I wanted to have breakfast later in town.

title: First time in Wrocław....a wonderful city, great electric cart tours and river cruises, I recommend...

Food added only after guests have requested it is unacceptable. A few tables and the staff doesn't notice that the guests' dishes need to be collected....It was poor and looked like young people were just learning... they were walking here and there without noticing what needed to be done, moving too quickly from one place to another without a purpose, which disturbs the peace that should accompany the meal, and the coffee was very bad. The reception deserves a plus - it is nice and professional.

title: Bad smell, stuffy, dysfunctional bathroom
pos_text:
Breakfast - large selection, lots of extras, lactose-free milk, quality meats and cheeses. There is plenty to choose from.

The staff was very friendly and helpful.
neg_text:
There is no air conditioning or any ventilation in the rooms, and on top of that there is an unpleasant smell inside...

Opening a window doesn't do much good when it's warm outside. A stay of several days in August was absolutely unbearable, the room was almost suffocating from the stuffiness. We ran away from it immediately after breakfast, because on top of everything else there was the nasty, musty smell of the old room.

The bathroom ventilation only worked for a minute or two after being turned on (so you would have to turn it on every now and then), but it didn't really give anything anyway. The toilet is cramped and uncomfortable.

The shower and sink drains are severely clogged.
The water pressure in the shower head, despite different times of showering, was constantly very, very weak, it was difficult to rinse the shampoo out properly.

No sockets by the beds, TV smaller than the advertising screens in supermarkets. Not that we need it, but it does complete the picture.

The cleaning crew does their job well, and the cleanliness is generally okay, but the facility is definitely showing signs of age and really needs a refresh.

title: Never again…
pos_text:
There was a tram stop nearby.
The bed was very comfortable and it was relatively clean.
neg_text: Hotel two days before arrival (date is early December) he sent information that they had a special occasion - a company Christmas Eve party. If I were to change the location, hotel prices at that time for such a close date cost at least PLN 500 per night (ignoring the problem of availability on weekends) - I come to Wrocław alone to study. I arrived before 10pm, tired after a full day of education. I think I spent over 20 minutes at the reception, even though I had already provided all the information on booking - invoice details, phone number, and I don't know what else the lady wrote there. I say they already have my number, she says she doesn't have a number. And the hotel called me 2 hours earlier... Typing speed slower than slow. She was supposed to send me the invoice by email right away. The music was blaring beneath me all night long, and the receptionist refused to move my room to a quieter area, even though there was a place on the opposite side of the room. At 4:30 I was woken up by the screams of a couple making love. And I don't think they were very loud at all, but the walls are very thin. A great night as before the next morning classes, waking up at 6:30. The bathroom was recently renovated, but the shower head was probably 20 years old. Hair stuck to the shower walls. But only 4. As if that wasn't enough, the hotel doesn't know how to issue invoices. I waited almost 3 weeks. There was another problem because they were supposedly issuing a correction for a week because someone made a mistake. They responded to messages with a delay of several days. And the atmosphere is a bit like the PRL. All together: a tragedy in its entirety.

title: I DO NOT RECOMMEND!!!! PLEASE READ THE DESCRIPTION!!!!!
pos_text: During a two-day stay at this hotel, the first day was ok, the service was very nice. Towels are changed regularly at the customer's request, cleaning products, coffee, tea and toilet paper are provided.
neg_text: During my second night at the hotel, in a rented apartment, I experienced an unpleasant adventure. Namely, in the morning while sleeping my bed collapsed (cornea) on which I slept when the children slept on the double bed. I immediately informed the hotel staff about this, expressed my dissatisfaction with this situation, asking for an appropriate approach to the customer in such a situation, taking into account the quite high cost of renting the apartment. After informing the hotel staff, I was informed that the owner of the facility would be informed about this situation and that the facility's cleaner would inspect the bed. Unfortunately, after 6 a.m., I was forced to return to the apartment without the possibility of further rest. On the floor where my apartment was located, a cleaning lady was working around 9 o'clock and I asked her to inspect the bed, which had collapsed. After a long time, after the cleaning lady had inspected it, I managed to put the damaged hinge from the extendable part of the cornea back in place and it was possible to fold it or unfold it again. At approximately 9:15 a.m., I was informed by the hotel staff at the reception that, after speaking with the owner, I would be charged PLN 400 for damage to the bed. During this conversation I also received information that the cornea is not intended for sleeping for adults. After informing the hotel staff that they would refer the case to a lawyer for treating me and my family in such a way, that a room with a damaged bed was rented for me and my family, endangering my health which I still feel in my lower back and that I had to pay for the repair of the already damaged bed. After this conversation, after a dozen or so minutes, I was informed by the hotel staff that the hotel owner would not want to charge me for the cost of repairing the damaged cornea.

title: Bad
pos_text: Breakfast was good. Nothing else!
neg_text:
The rooms have absolutely no soundproofing. An 18th birthday party was organized in a restaurant with a DJ, and the room on the 4th floor was filled with bass from 6:00 p.m. to 4:00 a.m. It was so loud that the bed started vibrating. The reception refused to move me to another room and gave me earplugs - despite this, everything was very loud. I didn't sleep the whole night and I came there to rest and get some sleep because I had training and an exam every day! I was exhausted mentally and physically. The manager refused when I demanded a refund for that sleepless night, explaining that she couldn't refund it because the room was sold, and she offered dinner at the restaurant as an apology, or a discount on another reservation at their hotel. I declined because I would definitely never book accommodation there again and I didn't need dinner because I had a training course!! I received information about the event on the day of check-in at 5:50 a.m. when I was already on my way to Wrocław for an all-day training. It said it might be louder and I could cancel or postpone my reservation free of charge, and I booked it over 2 months in advance!! Where was I supposed to quickly look for accommodation for 6 days after the training on the same day!!!!!
Zero soundproofing, on other nights you could hear a crying baby, a blaring TV, loud slamming doors, a shower, let alone a party with a DJ!! After entering the room, I noticed that the sheets on one bed were dirty with blood, I asked for a change for 2 days!!! The carpet in the room smelled of dirt and old age! The service at the reception was not friendly, when passing by they looked away to the computer, no smile on their face. The hotel is a disaster, it's not worth the money, and the manager claims that over the weekend they have more guests who are at parties and she doesn't care about the well-being of other guests! Either hotel guests or parties! Either soundproof the rooms or adapt the event hall for this purpose!!

pos_text: A hotel with very nice decor, also in the rooms. A very varied and tasty breakfast. Overall, the room was clean, and that's all there is to it.
neg_text:
Unfortunately, the passage of time is visible in this hotel. During our short stay, we found a few seemingly trivial things that effectively reduced the comfort of our stay:

- leaking shower with missing door seal. Additionally, the door is either damaged or has dropped, which is why the other seals are not closing properly. After every bath, there is just a puddle in front of the shower

- bathroom tiles moving on the floor when you step on them, or a damaged toilet seat, or rather its mounting.

- traces of mold in the shower cabin, the shower faucet is so calcified that it is difficult to set the water temperature

- bathroom fan not working, bathroom door handle loose,

- leaky window, although there are external blinds and it can be somewhat prevented from getting cold air in, but it's weak.

- a worn out bed mattress to the point that when you sleep you just sink in the middle

- no garbage can, except for a mini garbage can in the bathroom. There are tea cups and a kettle - but no spoons or anything to stir the tea with :)

title: The price does not match the quality :(
pos_text: The biggest advantage of this hotel is its location
neg_text: No drinking water in the room. The carpet is dirty - at first glance it looks clean, but there is a lot of dust in the corners, and the vacuuming was done very imprecisely. The parking lot was completely icy and unpaved, as was the hotel exit. Before 8:00 a.m. the grounds were literally an ice rink. This cannot be explained by weather conditions – it is simply negligence on the part of the facility. The lack of any security measures is unacceptable and poses a real threat to guests. This situation shouldn't happen

title: so-so, but well-rested
pos_text: Appearance of the building from the outside. Good hotel layout. There was a wedding in one wing, and in the other it did not disturb the rest of the guests.
neg_text: It is definitely not a premium hotel as its name suggests. It shouldn't be advertised like that. A hotel may try to be, but putting plastic ornate chandeliers everywhere doesn't make it premium. I work on the computer a lot - upon arrival, the internet in the room, which was advertised as "high-speed connection", was so slow that it was practically non-existent. After reporting this to reception it was better after an hour. However, I assume this is due to the fact that the other guests had gone to bed. This morning there was another problem with slow Internet. The breakfast rolls were from the previous day. The day before there was a wedding and for breakfast there were typical dinner dishes in the bain-marie. Additionally, the breakfast was rather average. Coffee machines could be replaced. The breakfast staff aggressively told other guests to be quieter because they were not alone (it could have been done in a 3-star hotel).

neg_text:
A hotel with potential, unfortunately leaves much to be desired. I arrived early and the room wasn't ready yet, and I don't blame them. I could leave my car in the hotel parking lot. A pleasant surprise is parking and breakfast included in the price.
I returned to the hotel in the evening after a full day of sightseeing in Wrocław. Service, hmm... I can't judge the guy who looked like he'd been hit by a train, or at least had just been running away from someone, and the rather less than elegant approach to customers seems to be the domain of young people. We received a card for the booked room. That's what we thought, and the only requirement was a double bed. Unfortunately, this too outgrew the hotel. We got 2 connected beds, with different sizes and hardness of mattresses. The carpet is not very clean, there is live chives in the tea, but the highlight is the dirty socks left on the shelf in the wardrobe.
To sum up, I do not recommend this hotel. Considering the cost of a round trip by Uber is 80 PLN, it is better to pay extra and have a better standard closer to the center of Wrocław.

There was supposed to be a room with a double bed - there were two single beds, there was supposed to be a bathtub - there was a tight shower. I couldn't find the refrigerator. Absolutely no free parking spaces. Perhaps it was the wedding guests, because upon entering the reception area I was greeted by the incredibly loud wailing of the groom. I prayed and was heard to have peace far from this... event. However, this resulted in several minutes of wandering around the rather convoluted hotel corridors with our luggage, looking for the correct room number. Well, tired with luggage, after driving 400 km and working many hours - I really had no strength left to investigate where the promised hotel stars were.

title: This hotel needs to change a lot. It seems to be fine. But the devil is in the details...
pos_text: It was the worst hotel I have ever stayed in. It's clear that no one is really interested in what impressions guests might have. After pointing out the old food, I was refunded for breakfast for the whole family, but it is impossible to unsee the corn gluten in my morning salad. I wouldn't eat another breakfast there. Of course, not everything was terrible, but a few very weak things spoiled the overall good impression.
neg_text: At breakfast they served old corn with slime.. dead greens for salads, water for tea barely reached 60 degrees, unless you changed the temperature settings yourself. The waffle batter was not coming out of the dispenser, I had to train the staff on how it works and what to do. There is a play area with an Xbox for children, but the sensor is set up so that only children 2 meters tall can use it. They were supposed to change it, but for two days nothing happened. In my children's room, they found paraphernalia for smoking marijuana or other drugs under the bed. The director was supposed to contact me after our visit, but I guess she forgot. Maybe after reading the reviews you will come to the conclusion that it is worth improving something and listening to the guests, especially since I expressed my comments in a delicate way. I do not recommend this hotel, I advise great caution during breakfast, there is definitely something wrong in the kitchen, in my opinion there is some systemic problem there.

title: Horrible experience, would never come again
pos_text:
The hotel is located away from the city center, which means it's pretty quiet outside.
The room itself is pretty large, nicely decorated and clean.
neg_text: There is a serious problem with internal sound proofing of the entire hotel. Seems like the air ducts from all the rooms are directly interconnected between them and also directly to the hotel lobby. That meant that, even though our room was on the 2nd floor (!!) and 50 meters away from the lobby (!!), we could clearly (!!) hear what people were talking inside the hotel lobby, up until 2 AM in the morning and starting again from 7AM, thus we couldn't properly rest. This could've been mitigated properly if the staff would've answered our initial booking message in which we specifically asked if they have problems with sound insulation and if they did, we could've cancelled our booking 4 months in advance. But they didn't respond at all. Furthermore, when confronted with the sound issues and the fact that we didn't sleep during the night and that we want to leave immediately, the staff didn't handle the situation promptly and wasted more of our time. We had to go through 2 managers and 3 rounds of explanations before they finally understood that we were not planning on staying another night and accepted to refund the remaining of the stay.

pos_text: The stay itself was fine. The breakfast was delicious and the number of dishes to choose from was impressive. The pool is basic, but perfectly adequate. Getting to the hotel is a bit confusing because it is connected to a KFC and a gas station.
neg_text: On the first night I encountered an absurd and unacceptable situation in which drunk guests were making such a terrible noise that they could be heard throughout the entire floor. Only personal intervention was able to silence this riffraff.

title: I recommend the hotel because of its location, delicious meals and satisfactory standard of rooms.
pos_text: Good location. Breakfast included in the price is tasty and with a large selection of dishes.
neg_text: Misunderstanding regarding so-called "special wishes". I requested the possibility of reserving a parking space in the reservation form (paid parking) and I indicated that I would arrive between 2:00 p.m. and 3:00 p.m. (the hotel check-in time is 2:00 p.m.). I received a reply via email that the staff would be happy to fulfill my "special request". Upon arrival, I was informed that the hotel does not take parking reservations, and the staff stated that my "special request" was to check in at 2:00 PM (!?). I will just add that there were a dozen or so spaces in the hotel parking lot with the description - "reserved space". This is some kind of misunderstanding. But eventually a parking space was found and that is good news. But what's the point of these "special requests" if, as a rule, they are not fulfilled and regular check-in during standard hours is treated as special treatment of guests.

title: Nice hotel, but in summer!
pos_text: Very nice hotel. A big plus was the cleanliness, the swimming pool and the location, which perfectly suited my needs during my stay. I also appreciate the proximity of shops and restaurants. This is the second time I have stayed at the hotel; my previous visit took place during the summer and I rated it very highly. Unfortunately, this time I have to lower my rating.
neg_text: The room was a real sauna. Air conditioning (according to the information on the plate located next to the control panel) in winter it only works in heating mode and despite being set to the lowest possible 16°C, it only heated the already warm room, and the window in the room cannot be opened or tilted. This is quite a surprise considering the amount you have to pay per night. Unfortunately, this made it difficult to fall asleep and caused constant waking up, which significantly affected the comfort of the stay - I wanted to run away from the room. If I decide to stay again, it will only be in the summer; however, in the winter I will look for another hotel with working air conditioning.

neg_text:
Several problems:
1) The room is much smaller than it appears from the photos and the bathroom, "ship service" sized, was right next to the bed and there was zero privacy.

2)The pillows provided were half-empty and incredibly uncomfortable, and there were no others in the room.
The duvets they provide are too heavy even with the air conditioning off and you end up sleeping half uncovered or sleeping in your underwear and feeling cold or in your pajamas and sweating.

3)Cleanliness needs to be reviewed, the lamp above the bathroom mirror had a layer of dust that had accumulated who knows how long ago.

4)More seriously, we stayed for three nights and they failed to clean the room every day, with the result that on the last day we had neither clean tablecloths nor toilet paper rolls.

5) the air conditioner was too powerful and even setting it to 22 degrees (the minimum) meant you were suffocating after a very short time and the lack of an opening window was a problem.

I understand that it is a conference hotel but here we don't even feel the passing grade.

The receptionists were not very pleasant and when I reported a problem with the air conditioning, the lady came in with obvious dissatisfaction.

title: Windows that don't open, noisy airflow and stuffy air at night
pos_text: If you have a car, there is good transport connection to the Old Town (10-15 minutes via the Warsaw Bridge). Nearby there is a KFC restaurant and a BP gas station. Hotel guests can use the small swimming pool free of charge, although only during specific hours (during our stay it was from 4:00 p.m. to 10:00 p.m.).
neg_text:
- The biggest disadvantage is the non-opening windows (probably to save on fire insurance) and an unreliable ventilation system that malfunctions if other guests on the same floor have their ventilation options set incorrectly. This caused quite a stuffy atmosphere on the first night, with hot air pouring in from the unopened window and the room temperature ranging between 25 and 32 degrees Celsius. C. The loud airflow turned off by itself after 2-3 minutes. It was already quiet at night and the facility staff were unable to check the settings in other guests' rooms. As a result of the intervention, we received an office fan and a promise that it would be repaired the next morning. To survive until morning, we had to sleep with the door ajar, risking having our luggage stolen.

- Extra paid parking, parking at the BP gas station is risky (only allowed up to max. 30 minutes under threat of towing).
- Equipment such as a kettle, glasses, cutlery or garbage bags are not standard, but you can easily ask for them at the reception.
- The pool is a bit small, designed for a maximum of 6 people. Then it gets too crowded and competitive swimming, especially crawl, becomes impossible for fear of hitting other guests.

- Breakfast additionally payable PLN 45 - standard offer without fireworks, fine for max. 3 mornings. Then, due to the repetition of the offer, monotony creeps in. There are no large plates, so you have to go to the buffet several times with small plates.

The underground parking was 10 PLN more, and the lady told me it was only 10 PLN, but for example, for 10 days it would be 100 PLN, and the information on the website says 40 PLN for a passenger car and 60 PLN for a van, and here the lady charges me 50 PLN and threatens me that if there is a stain on the towel, I will have to pay 40 PLN. God, I'm afraid to touch anything in this hotel. I will definitely never use this hotel again.

Podczas całego pobytu w pokoju panowała bardzo niska temperatura, ponieważ hotel nie był przygotowany na sezon grzewczy. Informację tę otrzymałam bezpośrednio od pracownicy recepcji, która przy zgłoszeniu problemu stwierdziła, że hotel „po prostu nie jest przystosowany na sezon grzewczy” i nie można włączyć ogrzewania. W nocy było tak zimno, że musiałam spać pod dwiema kołdrami i w ubraniu, a włączony grzejnik w łazience nie ogrzał pokoju. Po zgłoszeniu problemu w recepcji otrzymałam jedynie stary, przenośny grzejnik, który był rozwiązaniem prowizorycznym i niewystarczającym.

Uważam, że brak ogrzewania w listopadzie stanowi poważne naruszenie standardów hotelowych i minimalnego komfortu pobytu.

Dodatkowo pani rejestratorka bardzo bała się policji, iż będą sprawdzane kamery i t.d, powiedziała czy mogłabym zadzwonić do znajomych, żeby przywieźli mi dokument(prawie 22 oraz 400km od domu). title: Okropna obsługa i podejście do klijebta!!!
pos_text: Początkowa cena oraz udogodnienia.
neg_text:
Jest to jeden z najgorszych hoteli pod względem podejścia do klienta który spotkałem w życiu.
Po rezerwacji i dokonaniu opłaty za pobyt dostałem telefon od hotelu z informacją, że do ceny którą już zapłaciłem muszę dopłacić dodatkową kwotę za pobyt dziecka, które pod czas rezerwacji było wliczone w cenę. Na pytanie dlaczego taka informacja pojawia się dopiero po dokonaniu rezerwacji, obsługa powiedziała, że booking.com liczy inaczej, a oni inaczej. Kiedy poprosiłem skasować rezerwację i zwrócić pieniądze, by mógł znaleźć inne miejcie menadżer Anna powiedziała, że nie ma takiej opcji i chamsko żucia słuchawkę, kończąc rozmowę. W regulaminie hotelu nie ma info jak oni liczą pobyt dziecka, natomiast podobno umieścili ową informacje na zdjęciach pokoi, co uważam za wprowadzenie Klijnstra w błąd. Radzę uważać na to miejsce. Ze swojej strony, napewno drugi raz już tutaj się nie pojawię.

Було замовлено дитяче ліжечко але по приїзду його не було, принесли згодом через дуже великі прохання. У нас була дуже шумна сторона, дорога, трамваї. Не рекомендую.

title: Das Verhalten des Hotelpersonals war äußerst unangenehm und wenig sympathisch.
pos_text: Nichts hat besonders gefallen – vom Service bis zur Ausstattung war alles mehr als schlecht
neg_text:
Der Service war inakzeptabel – als Gast fühlte man sich weder willkommen noch ernst genommen.
Bei auftretenden Problemen wurde nicht lösungsorientiert reagiert. Stattdessen wurde Druck ausgeübt, um berechtigte Kritik zu unterdrücken. Leider betraf dieses Verhalten nicht nur eine einzelne Person, sondern war bei mehreren Mitarbeitenden zu beobachten.

Das Personal zeigt keine Bereitschaft, kundenorientiert auf Probleme einzugehen. Statt aktiver Unterstützung entstand der Eindruck, dass man sich hinter der Rezeption versteckte.

Besonders unverständlich war die Änderung meiner Buchung:
Obwohl ich ausdrücklich ein Zimmer mit Frühstück reserviert hatte, wurde mir ein kleineres Zimmer ohne Frühstück zugewiesen – ohne Angabe von Gründen. Aufgrund fehlender Englischkenntnisse beim Personal war es zudem nicht möglich, eine nachvollziehbare Erklärung einzufordern. Stattdessen wurde mit Aussagen wie „Wir haben kein Zimmer für Sie!“ oder „Wir können keine zusätzliche Bettwäsche anbieten – das geht nicht!“ reagiert – selbst in Fällen, in denen ich bereits für die gewünschte Leistung bezahlt habe.

Aufgrund der ursprünglich viel zu kleinen Zimmer für eine Familie war ein Zimmerwechsel vor Ort notwendig verbunden mit einer zusätzlichen Zahlung. Selbst dieser organisatorische Vorgang gestaltete sich äußerst kompliziert: Die Aussagen der Mitarbeitenden widersprachen sich erheblich, sodass der gesamte Prozess nervenaufreibend war.

Bereits bei einfachen Bitten, wie der Bitte, den Frühstückstisch abzuwischen, da noch deutlich sichtbare Spuren vom vorherigen Gast und Haare vorhanden waren, zeigte sich das Personal unaufmerksam. Erst nach mehrmaligem Nachfragen wurde die Bitte halbherzig erfüllt – freundlich war der Umgangston dabei keineswegs.

Besonders irritierend war schließlich, dass man nach dem Aufenthalt eine separate Nachricht vom Hotel erhielt – mit der Bitte um eine gute Bewertung.

Das Frühstück war unspektakulär – die vielen Top-Bewertungen kann nicht nachvollziehen!

title: The room is unfit for use
pos_text: We managed to get a full refund
neg_text:
The room is unfit for habitation. A damaged lock meant there was a risk of being unable to escape in the event of an emergency. The staff provided no alternative and were unhelpful.
Moreover, the room did not match the description - instead of a double bed, two beds simply placed next to each other, a hole between the mattresses, and no physical connection between the beds meant that one could unexpectedly end up sleeping on the floor. Two beds next to each other are NOT a double bed, and if a hotel insists they don't want such beds, they should at least make sure they accommodate them!

title: If I may advise, for your stay in Wroclaw, definitely choose another hotel for the sake of your health.
neg_text:
Upon arriving at the hotel, I was very unpleasantly surprised that the window in the room could not be opened. The temperature in the room was about 25 degrees. After asking at the reception, I was told that this was to prevent guests from jumping out of the window and that I should use the air conditioning. I was unable to adjust the air conditioning to lower the temperature, instead it reached 30 degrees within a few minutes. After further inquiry at the reception, I was told that the air conditioning cannot be set to cooling during the winter months, only to heating. When I asked (in English) how I could lower the temperature in the room, as it was impossible to sleep peacefully at 28 degrees, the receptionist replied that we were in Poland, and therefore she would only speak Polish to me. However, within the next few sentences, she smoothly switched to Ukrainian, probably because I couldn't understand her. Still, she didn't give me any advice.
I find the receptionist's approach completely unprofessional and discouraging.
She is probably employed there to discourage guests from asking unpleasant questions at the reception.
The sleep at night was very restless, it was impossible to breathe peacefully, and in the morning I woke up with a severe headache. There was no question of rest.
When I realize that the room has no ventilation, and the air in the room has certainly been the same for the last few years, I can be glad that I didn't end up with some serious problem in the care of healthcare professionals. This can be a very serious problem for allergy sufferers.
I have stayed in about 16 hotels across Europe over the last two years, but the experience at this hotel is clearly the worst.

title: Remove from filter as the property does not provide parking as it is listed in the filter.
pos_text: I arrived a day earlier trying to clarify the parking information, because when choosing a hotel on booking.com I filtered out hotels with parking; I had valuable items in the car and it turned out that after paying, they sent information about the parking being first come, first served. Well, I went a day earlier and the staff didn't want to try to solve the problem, maybe somehow reserve 1 place exceptionally? Unfortunately, complete ignorance and the excuse that there is such a note in the hotel description. So why these filters! So maybe when I book a room with two beds and set up such a filter, I should look for the small print somewhere - if you arrive late, it might only be a double :( A day earlier I personally informed the reception that I would not be arriving because the hotel did not meet my expectations and I am withdrawing from the contract. I wrote this many times in correspondence with the hotel. Unfortunately, they absolutely do not understand my arguments. This has greatly affected my perception of the booking platform because filters exist to help me specify my expectations. Setting up a filter and then reading often long descriptions of properties and looking for some loopholes and systems that exclude them, or informing after payment that there is parking but in fact there isn't because if you arrive too late it might not be available, is, in my opinion, just plain cheating. To sum up, a day earlier I personally tried to ask for a reservation for special reasons, unfortunately there was complete ignorance both in person and in writing. Personally, I expect my money back out of principle. Jacek Kryger

title: I have never encountered such incompetent and arrogant hotel staff.
pos_text: It's hard to say if there was anything I liked, except maybe the breakfast buffet, which is a great advantage of the facility. The hotel is quite average, the rooms are rather cramped. Purely.
neg_text:
Approach to the stay of animals on the premises.
The hotel accepts them and does not charge a fee for the stay. A deposit is collected upon check-in, to be settled in the event of damage caused by animals (PLN 100). This seemed clear and understandable until I left and asked for an accounting of my stay. The first surprise was that we were asked to leave the room for the duration of the inspection and assessment."destruction" was done without our presence. And what I heard knocked me off my feet. The dog slept in the bed and the room needs a thorough cleaning. I know many owners let their pets sleep on their beds. We, however, are strongly opposed to this. So, in protest, I asked for another verification, this time in my presence. And so the previous, already rather rude lady was joined by her boss, just as unpleasant as the previous one, and together they inspected the room. With detective-like thoroughness they searched the bed and the surrounding area. Three black hairs were found on the bedsheets and a dozen or so on the armchair. Nothing else.
On this basis, we were charged with contaminating the room, which consequently required the hotel staff to "engage additional resources, both personnel and technical, to remove what they called the devastation of the room." Instead of a refund of the deposit, I received a bill for the damage to the hotel room in the amount of PLN 100.
Unfortunately, during the whole commotion, I lost my temper due to the absurdity of the whole incident and the discussion ended in a fight. This is how my stay at the Hotel Weiser ended.
I definitely do not recommend it, especially for those traveling with animals.
If someone from the Management Board is reading this and the animal's stay is actually that problematic for you. Please consider introducing fees instead of tormenting guests with such scenes. It will be simple, honest. If, however, you are patching up this is the hotel's financial way, I wish you good luck, because it does not bode well.

title: The value for money was very poor!
pos_text: The pluses are definitely the location, friendly staff, a comfortable bed with separate duvets, and free water in the hallway. These were the only advantages of the stay.
neg_text: Unfortunately, there are a lot of things that could be improved here. The hotel is clearly old and it shows in almost everything – from the furnishings to the overall standard. The gym was very small and on the second day the door broke and we couldn't use it. The bathrobes were old, one even had a hole in it. The Wi-Fi was very poor, the TV kept freezing up, and most of the channels were German, making the hotel seem heavily geared towards German guests. The food prices were definitely exaggerated considering the quality. Unfortunately, breakfast was disappointing, with little choice, average taste and no allergen labeling. Additionally, the bathroom was not very comfortable, the toilet was very low (for a child) , and after lifting the flap it was not possible to drain the water normally. At this price and for a 4* hotel we expected a much higher standard

My biggest reservations are about the restaurant's daytime operation – the waitresses require additional training in guest service. Despite having free tables, we were refused a seat several times, and the same thing happened again the next day. The impression was that the staff were focused primarily on the German coach tour, ignoring individual guests.

Additional difficulties were the queues at the coffee machines and the crowded breakfast room, which seemed too small for the number of reservations.

The hotel has potential, but the organization and service in the restaurant definitely need improvement.

title: Expectations were not met.
pos_text:
The location is very good. Many things are within walking distance. Some of the staff even speak German and, with a few exceptions, are very friendly.
The restaurant and lobby bar are good. The view from the room was beautiful; you could see a lot.
neg_text:
The price-performance ratio is not really balanced. There was a loud fan in the room that could not be turned off. Furthermore, the windows could not be opened, which made the room stuffy. The mattress in the room was extremely worn out. The shower door in the bathroom was crooked, causing water to leak into the bathroom. A bracket inside the shower itself had broken off.
It was also a shame that the breakfast buffet was not fully set up at 8 a.m. on a Sunday.

title: Poorly
pos_text: Location and view of the Odra River.
neg_text:
The building has long since seen its glory days. The hallways reek of old, dusty carpeting.
As for the room itself: no way to open the windows = no fresh air. There is air conditioning, but it was too cold to turn it on during our stay. But it wouldn't be so good... We had no fresh air, but in the morning we woke up with a runny nose and a sore throat because the bathroom ventilation was blowing cold air anyway.
Bathroom: remembers the times of Wałęsa. Pseudo-marble, which is supposed to simulate wealth and prestige, but in the joints there is ordinary fungus and mold. The bathtub was not cleaned properly and had a yellow stain. We wondered if it was safe to bathe in it. Standard like from the Middle East. The glass next to the bathtub pretending to be a shower - a very unsuccessful idea. Despite very careful cleaning, the entire bathroom was flooded with water. There is a sewer smell in the bathroom. The smell is receding from the drains.
Breakfasts are completely average for 70 PLN/person. Fruit salad with canned fruit. It reeks of the previous decade. Overall a strong 3 out of 10 just for the location and staff. We will definitely not visit again.
PS.: Very soft mattresses. It's a matter of taste, but we didn't sleep even for an hour. The bed moved with every movement.

title: it's hard to say
pos_text: The breakfast was varied and very tasty, the bed was comfortable, the view was beautiful, and the older gentleman serving was extremely polite - I don't know his position, but he was the one who turned on the lamps downstairs in the hall.
neg_text: After arriving at the hotel, it turned out that our knowledge about the fee for the stay was significantly different from what we had when we arrived there. The final price quoted by booking was different from the one we were supposed to pay, which surprised us because no one at the hotel bothered to recalculate and provide the current price. Then one of the booked rooms (the only one with higher standards), right from the start, refused to cooperate and blew the plugs, so we were left sitting in complete darkness until the porter arrived. Then it turned out that the bedside lamp was not working and there was no bottle of water in the room. The room also had other shortcomings - dirty walls, broken hangers, and no handle to close the shower door. There is an unpleasant smell in the room, the windows cannot be opened, so the air conditioning has to run constantly, and its noise does not make it easier to sleep, and the effect of its operation does not allow the room to be aired. Very unfriendly staff at the reception who want to prove their point at all costs, without any reflection or polite explanation of the whole situation. Besides, if I booked the rooms and paid for them, I don't understand the idea of showing a different ID card for each room. It so happened that my 80-year-old mother was with us, whose birthday we were supposed to celebrate there. She didn't take any documents because she wasn't obliged to do so and knew she would be traveling with us, and instead of arranging this matter so that the elderly lady could rest after the rigors of the journey, she waved the hotel regulations in front of us. Additionally, on the second day of our stay, our room was not cleaned and late in the evening I had to ask for at least the cups to be replaced with clean ones and for water and coffee to be brought, not to mention the fact that we had to pick up dirty towels from the floor to dry ourselves with after using the shower.It is a pity that the hotel neglects its customers by not informing them about many issues, which causes frustration, and the birthday stay, instead of being a joy, turns into a nightmare
